children.text: Each carrier with a contract in effect under this part with respect to suppliers of covered items shall send any notice of denial of payment for covered items by reason of paragraph (17)(B) and for which payment is not requested on an assignment-related basis to the supplier and the patient involved.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a supplier who does not request reconsideration or seek appeal on a timely basis, the refund is made within 30 days after the date the supplier receives a denial notice under subparagraph (C), or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case in which such a reconsideration or appeal is taken, the refund is made within 15 days after the date the supplier receives notice of an adverse determination on reconsideration or appeal.

children.heading: Individual’s right to choose upgraded item
children.label: A Individual’s right to choose upgraded item
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, the Secretary may issue regulations under which an individual may purchase or rent from a supplier an item of upgraded durable medical equipment for which payment would be made under this subsection if the item were a standard item.

children.label: i
children.text: the supplier shall receive payment under this subsection with respect to such item as if such item were a standard item; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual purchasing or renting the item shall pay the supplier an amount equal to the difference between the supplier’s charge and the amount under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: determination of fair market prices with respect to an upgraded item;

children.label: ii
children.text: full disclosure of the availability and price of standard items and proof of receipt of such disclosure information by the beneficiary before the furnishing of the upgraded item;

children.label: iii
children.text: conditions of participation for suppliers in the billing arrangement;

children.label: iv
children.text: sanctions of suppliers who are determined to engage in coercive or abusive practices, including exclusion; and

children.label: v
children.text: such other safeguards as the Secretary determines are necessary.

children.label: i
children.text: furnish any such item or service for which payment is made under this part; and

children.label: ii
children.text: receive or retain a provider or supplier number used to submit claims for reimbursement for any such item or service for which payment may be made under this subchapter.

children.heading: Designation of independent accreditation organizations
children.label: B Designation of independent accreditation organizations
children.text: Not later than the date that is 1 year after the date on which the Secretary implements the quality standards under subparagraph (A), notwithstanding , the Secretary shall designate and approve one or more independent accreditation organizations for purposes of such subparagraph.section 1395bb(a) of this title

children.heading: Quality standards
children.label: C Quality standards
children.text: The quality standards described in subparagraph (A) may not be less stringent than the quality standards that would otherwise apply if this paragraph did not apply and shall include consumer services standards.

children.label: i
children.text: Covered items (as defined in paragraph (13)) for which payment may otherwise be made under this subsection.

children.label: ii
children.text: Prosthetic devices and orthotics and prosthetics described in subsection (h)(4).

children.label: iii
children.text: Items and services described in .section 1395u(s)(2) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: Lymphedema compression treatment items (as defined in ).section 1395x(mmm) of this title

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: E Implementation
children.text: The Secretary may establish by program instruction or otherwise the quality standards under this paragraph, including subparagraph (F), after consultation with representatives of relevant parties. Such standards shall be applied prospectively and shall be published on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.label: i
children.text: subject to clause (ii) and subparagraph (G), the Secretary shall require suppliers furnishing items and services described in subparagraph (D) on or after , directly or as a subcontractor for another entity, to have submitted to the Secretary evidence of accreditation by an accreditation organization designated under subparagraph (B) as meeting applicable quality standards, except that the Secretary shall not require under this clause pharmacies to obtain such accreditation before , except that the Secretary shall not require a pharmacy to have submitted to the Secretary such evidence of accreditation prior to ; andOctober 1, 2009January 1, 2010January 1, 2011

children.label: I
children.text: such standards and accreditation requirement shall not apply to such professionals and persons unless the Secretary determines that the standards being applied are designed specifically to be applied to such professionals and persons; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary may exempt such professionals and persons from such standards and requirement if the Secretary determines that licensing, accreditation, or other mandatory quality requirements apply to such professionals and persons with respect to the furnishing of such items and services.

children.heading: Application of accreditation requirement to certain pharmacies
children.label: G Application of accreditation requirement to certain pharmacies
I
children.text: subject to subclause (II), in applying such standards and the accreditation requirement of subparagraph (F)(i) with respect to pharmacies described in clause (ii) furnishing such items and services, such standards and accreditation requirement shall not apply to such pharmacies; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary may apply to such pharmacies an alternative accreditation requirement established by the Secretary if the Secretary determines such alternative accreditation requirement is more appropriate for such pharmacies.

children.label: I
children.text: The total billings by the pharmacy for such items and services under this subchapter are less than 5 percent of total pharmacy sales, as determined based on the average total pharmacy sales for the previous 3 calendar years, 3 fiscal years, or other yearly period specified by the Secretary.

children.label: II
children.text: The pharmacy has been enrolled under as a supplier of durable medical equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, and supplies, has been issued (which may include the renewal of) a provider number for at least 5 years, and for which a final adverse action (as defined in section 424.57(a) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations) has not been imposed in the past 5 years.section 1395cc(j) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: The pharmacy submits to the Secretary an attestation, in a form and manner, and at a time, specified by the Secretary, that the pharmacy meets the criteria described in subclauses (I) and (II). Such attestation shall be subject to .section 1001 of title 18

children.label: IV
children.text: The pharmacy agrees to submit materials as requested by the Secretary, or during the course of an audit conducted on a random sample of pharmacies selected annually, to verify that the pharmacy meets the criteria described in subclauses (I) and (II). Materials submitted under the preceding sentence shall include a certification by an accountant on behalf of the pharmacy or the submission of tax returns filed by the pharmacy during the relevant periods, as requested by the Secretary.

children.heading: Special payment rule for specified items and supplies
children.label: 21 Special payment rule for specified items and supplies
i
children.text: the amount of payment otherwise determined for the specified item or supply under this subsection for 2002, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of payment for the specified item or supply under chapter 89 of title 5, as identified in the column entitled “Median FEHP Price” in the table entitled “SUMMARY OF MEDICARE PRICES COMPARED TO VA, MEDICAID, RETAIL, AND FEHP PRICES FOR 16 ITEMS” included in the Testimony of the Inspector General before the Senate Committee on Appropriations, , or any subsequent report by the Inspector General.June 12, 2002

children.heading: Specified item or supply described
children.label: B Specified item or supply described
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a specified item or supply means oxygen and oxygen equipment, standard wheelchairs (including standard power wheelchairs), nebulizers, diabetic supplies consisting of lancets and testing strips, hospital beds, and air mattresses, but only if the HCPCS code for the item or supply is identified in a table referred to in subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Application of update to special payment amount
children.label: C Application of update to special payment amount
children.text: The covered item update under paragraph (14) for specified items and supplies for 2006 and each subsequent year shall be applied to the payment amount under subparagraph (A) unless payment is made for such items and supplies under .section 1395w–3 of this title

children.heading: Special payment rule for diabetic supplies
children.label: 22 Special payment rule for diabetic supplies
children.text: Notwithstanding the preceding provisions of this subsection, for purposes of determining the payment amount under this subsection for diabetic supplies furnished on or after the first day of the calendar quarter during 2013 that is at least 30 days after , and before the date described in paragraph (1)(H)(ii), the Secretary shall recalculate and apply the covered item update under paragraph (14) as if subparagraph (J)(i) of such paragraph was amended by striking “but only if furnished through mail order”.January 2, 2013

children.heading: Master List inclusion and claim review for certain items
children.label: 23 Master List inclusion and claim review for certain items

children.heading: Master List inclusion
children.label: A Master List inclusion
children.text: Beginning , for purposes of the Master List described in section 414.234(b) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation), in determining which items have aberrant billing patterns (as such term is used for purposes of such section), the Secretary shall also treat an item for which payment may be made under this subsection as having such an aberrant billing pattern if the Secretary determines that, without explanatory contributing factors (such as furnishing emergent care services), a substantial number of claims for such items under this subsection are for such items ordered by a physician or practitioner who has not previously (during a period of not less than 24 months, as established by the Secretary) furnished to the individual involved any item or service for which payment may be made under this subchapter.January 1, 2029

children.heading: Claim review
children.label: B Claim review
children.text: With respect to items furnished on or after , that are included on the Master List pursuant to subparagraph (A), if such an item is not subject to a determination of coverage in advance pursuant to paragraph (15)(C), the Secretary may conduct prepayment review of claims for payment for such item.January 1, 2029

children.heading: Fee schedules for radiologist services
children.label: b Fee schedules for radiologist services
A
children.text: a relative value scale to serve as the basis for the payment for radiologist services under this part, and

children.label: B
children.text: using such scale and appropriate conversion factors and subject to subsection (c)(1)(A), fee schedules (on a regional, statewide, locality, or carrier service area basis) for payment for radiologist services under this part, to be implemented for such services furnished during 1989.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Secretary shall regularly consult closely with the Physician Payment Review Commission, the American College of Radiology, and other organizations representing physicians or suppliers who furnish radiologist services and shall share with them the data and data analysis being used to make the determinations under paragraph (1), including data on variations in current medicare payments by geographic area, and by service and physician specialty.

children.label: A
children.text: shall take into consideration variations in the cost of furnishing such services among geographic areas and among different sites where services are furnished, and

children.label: B
children.text: may also take into consideration such other factors respecting the manner in which physicians in different specialties furnish such services as may be appropriate to assure that payment amounts are equitable and designed to promote effective and efficient provision of radiologist services by physicians in the different specialties.

children.heading: Budget neutral fee schedules
children.label: A Budget neutral fee schedules
children.text: The Secretary shall develop preliminary fee schedules for 1989, which are designed to result in the same amount of aggregate payments (net of any coinsurance and deductibles under sections 1395(a)(1)(J) and 1395(b) of this title) for radiologist services furnished in 1989 as would have been made if this subsection had not been enacted.ll

children.heading: Initial savings
children.label: B Initial savings
children.text: The fee schedules established for payment purposes under this subsection for services furnished in 1989 shall be 97 percent of the amounts permitted under the preliminary fee schedules developed under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: 1990 fee schedules
children.label: C 1990 fee schedules
children.text: For radiologist services (other than portable X-ray services) furnished under this part during 1990, after March 31 of such year, the conversion factors used under this subsection shall be 96 percent of the conversion factors that applied under this subsection as of .December 31, 1989

children.heading: National weighted average conversion factor
children.label: i National weighted average conversion factor
children.text: The Secretary shall estimate the national weighted average of the conversion factors used under this subsection for services furnished during 1990 beginning on April 1, using the best available data.

children.heading: Reduced national weighted average
children.label: ii Reduced national weighted average
children.text: The national weighted average estimated under clause (i) shall be reduced by 13 percent.

children.heading: Computation of 1990 locality index relative to national average
children.label: iii Computation of 1990 locality index relative to national average
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an index which reflects, for each locality, the ratio of the conversion factor used in the locality under this subsection to the national weighted average estimated under clause (i).

children.heading: Adjusted conversion factor
children.label: iv Adjusted conversion factor
children.text: The adjusted conversion factor for the professional or technical component of a service in a locality is the sum of ½ of the locally-adjusted amount determined under clause (v) and ½ of the GPCI-adjusted amount determined under clause (vi).

children.heading: Locally-adjusted amount
children.label: v Locally-adjusted amount
children.text: For purposes of clause (iv), the locally adjusted amount determined under this clause is the product of (I) the national weighted average conversion factor computed under clause (ii), and (II) the index value established under clause (iii) for the locality.

children.label: I
children.text: the product of (a) the portion of the reduced national weighted average conversion factor computed under clause (ii) which is attributable to physician work and (b) the geographic work index value for the locality (specified in Addendum C to the Model Fee Schedule for Physician Services (published on , 55 Federal Register pp. 36238–36243)); andSeptember 4, 1990

children.label: II
children.text: the product of (a) the remaining portion of the reduced national weighted average conversion factor computed under clause (ii), and (b) the geographic practice cost index value specified in for the locality.section 1395u(b)(14)(C)(iv) of this title

children.heading: Limits on conversion factor
children.label: vii Limits on conversion factor
children.text: The conversion factor to be applied to a locality to the professional or technical component of a service shall not be reduced under this subparagraph by more than 9.5 percent below the conversion factor applied in the locality under subparagraph (C) to such component, but in no case shall the conversion factor be less than 60 percent of the national weighted average of the conversion factors (computed under clause (i)).

children.heading: Rule for certain scanning services
children.label: E Rule for certain scanning services
children.text: In the case of the technical components of magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) services and computer assisted tomography (CAT) services furnished after , the amount otherwise payable shall be reduced by 10 percent.December 31, 1990

children.heading: Subsequent updating
children.label: F Subsequent updating
children.text: For radiologist services furnished in subsequent years, the fee schedules shall be the schedules for the previous year updated by the percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in ) for the year.section 1395u(i)(3) of this title

children.heading: Nonparticipating physicians and suppliers
children.label: G Nonparticipating physicians and suppliers
children.text: Each fee schedule so established shall provide that the payment rate recognized for nonparticipating physicians and suppliers is equal to the appropriate percent (as defined in ) of the payment rate recognized for participating physicians and suppliers.section 1395u(b)(4)(A)(iv) of this title

children.heading: Limiting charges of nonparticipating physicians and suppliers
children.label: 5 Limiting charges of nonparticipating physicians and suppliers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of radiologist services furnished after , for which payment is made under a fee schedule under this subsection, if a nonparticipating physician or supplier furnishes the service to an individual entitled to benefits under this part, the physician or supplier may not charge the individual more than the limiting charge (as defined in subparagraph (B)).January 1, 1989

children.label: i
children.text: in 1989, 125 percent of the amount specified for the service in the appropriate fee schedule established under paragraph (1),

children.label: ii
children.text: in 1990, 120 percent of the amount specified for the service in the appropriate fee schedule established under paragraph (1), and

children.label: iii
children.text: after 1990, 115 percent of the amount specified for the service in the appropriate fee schedule established under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: C Enforcement
children.text: If a physician or supplier knowingly and willfully bills in violation of subparagraph (A), the Secretary may apply sanctions against such physician or supplier in accordance with in the same manner as such sanctions may apply to a physician.section 1395u(j)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: who is certified, or eligible to be certified, by the American Board of Radiology, or

children.label: B
children.text: for whom radiology services account for at least 50 percent of the total amount of charges made under this part.

children.heading: Payment and standards for screening mammography
children.label: c Payment and standards for screening mammography
A
children.text: for screening mammography conducted consistent with the frequency permitted under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: if the screening mammography is conducted by a facility that has a certificate (or provisional certificate) issued under .section 263b of this title

children.label: i
children.text: no payment may be made under this part for screening mammography performed on a woman under 35 years of age;

children.label: ii
children.text: payment may be made under this part for only one screening mammography performed on a woman over 34 years of age, but under 40 years of age; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a woman over 39 years of age, payment may not be made under this part for screening mammography performed within 11 months following the month in which a previous screening mammography was performed.

children.heading: Review
children.label: i Review
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Director of the National Cancer Institute, shall review periodically the appropriate frequency for performing screening mammography, based on age and such other factors as the Secretary believes to be pertinent.

children.heading: Revision of frequency
children.label: ii Revision of frequency
children.text: The Secretary, taking into consideration the review made under clause (i), may revise from time to time the frequency with which screening mammography may be paid for under this subsection.

children.heading: Frequency limits and payment for colorectal cancer screening tests
children.label: d Frequency limits and payment for colorectal cancer screening tests

children.heading: Payment amount
children.label: A Payment amount
children.text: The payment amount for colorectal cancer screening tests consisting of screening fecal-occult blood tests is equal to the payment amount established for diagnostic fecal-occult blood tests under section 1395(h) of this title.l

children.label: i
children.text: if the individual is under 50 years of age; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the test is performed within the 11 months after a previous screening fecal-occult blood test.

children.heading: Fee schedule
children.label: A Fee schedule
children.text: With respect to colorectal cancer screening tests consisting of screening flexible sigmoidoscopies, payment under shall be consistent with payment under such section for similar or related services.section 1395w–4 of this title

children.heading: Payment limit
children.label: B Payment limit
children.text: In the case of screening flexible sigmoidoscopy services, payment under this part shall not exceed such amount as the Secretary specifies, based upon the rates recognized for diagnostic flexible sigmoidoscopy services.

children.label: I
children.text: in accordance with regulations, may be performed in an ambulatory surgical center and for which the Secretary permits ambulatory surgical center payments under this part, and

children.label: II
children.text: are performed in an ambulatory surgical center or hospital outpatient department,

children.label: I
children.text: in computing the amount of any applicable copayment, the computation of such coinsurance shall be based upon the fee schedule under which payment is made for the services, and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of such coinsurance is equal to 25 percent of the payment amount under the fee schedule described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Special rule for detected lesions
children.label: D Special rule for detected lesions
children.text: Subject to section 1395(a)(1)(Y) of this title, if during the course of such screening flexible sigmoidoscopy, a lesion or growth is detected which results in a biopsy or removal of the lesion or growth, payment under this part shall not be made for the screening flexible sigmoidoscopy but shall be made for the procedure classified as a flexible sigmoidoscopy with such biopsy or removal.l

children.label: i
children.text: if the individual is under 50 years of age; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the procedure is performed within the 47 months after a previous screening flexible sigmoidoscopy or, in the case of an individual who is not at high risk for colorectal cancer, if the procedure is performed within the 119 months after a previous screening colonoscopy.

children.heading: Fee schedule
children.label: A Fee schedule
children.text: With respect to colorectal cancer screening test consisting of a screening colonoscopy, payment under shall be consistent with payment amounts under such section for similar or related services.section 1395w–4 of this title

children.heading: Payment limit
children.label: B Payment limit
children.text: In the case of screening colonoscopy services, payment under this part shall not exceed such amount as the Secretary specifies, based upon the rates recognized for diagnostic colonoscopy services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subsections (i)(2)(A) and (t) of section 1395 of this title, in the case of screening colonoscopy services furnished on or after , that are performed in an ambulatory surgical center or a hospital outpatient department, payment under this part shall be based on the lesser of the amount under the fee schedule that would apply to such services if they were performed in a hospital outpatient department in an area or the amount under the fee schedule that would apply to such services if they were performed in an ambulatory surgical center in the same area.lJanuary 1, 1999

children.label: I
children.text: in computing the amount of any applicable coinsurance, the computation of such coinsurance shall be based upon the fee schedule under which payment is made for the services, and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of such coinsurance is equal to 25 percent of the payment amount under the fee schedule described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Special rule for detected lesions
children.label: D Special rule for detected lesions
children.text: Subject to section 1395(a)(1)(Y) of this title, if during the course of such screening colonoscopy, a lesion or growth is detected which results in a biopsy or removal of the lesion or growth, payment under this part shall not be made for the screening colonoscopy but shall be made for the procedure classified as a colonoscopy with such biopsy or removal.l

children.heading: Frequency limit
children.label: E Frequency limit
children.text: No payment may be made under this part for a colorectal cancer screening test consisting of a screening colonoscopy for individuals at high risk for colorectal cancer if the procedure is performed within the 23 months after a previous screening colonoscopy or for other individuals if the procedure is performed within the 119 months after a previous screening colonoscopy or within 47 months after a previous screening flexible sigmoidoscopy.

children.heading: Accreditation requirement for advanced diagnostic imaging services
children.label: e Accreditation requirement for advanced diagnostic imaging services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning with , with respect to the technical component of advanced diagnostic imaging services for which payment is made under the fee schedule established under and that are furnished by a supplier, payment may only be made if such supplier is accredited by an accreditation organization designated by the Secretary under paragraph (2)(B)(i).January 1, 2012section 1395w–4(b) of this title2So in original. Subpar. (B) of par. (2) does not contain clauses.2

children.heading: Advanced diagnostic imaging services defined
children.label: B Advanced diagnostic imaging services defined
i
children.text: diagnostic magnetic resonance imaging, computed tomography, and nuclear medicine (including positron emission tomography); and

children.label: ii
children.text: such other diagnostic imaging services, including services described in (excluding X-ray, ultrasound, and fluoroscopy), as specified by the Secretary in consultation with physician specialty organizations and other stakeholders.section 1395w–4(b)(4)(B) of this title

children.heading: Supplier defined
children.label: C Supplier defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “supplier” has the meaning given such term in .section 1395x(d) of this title

children.heading: Factors for designation of accreditation organizations
children.label: A Factors for designation of accreditation organizations
i
children.text: The ability of the organization to conduct timely reviews of accreditation applications.

children.label: ii
children.text: Whether the organization has established a process for the timely integration of new advanced diagnostic imaging services into the organization’s accreditation program.

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether the organization uses random site visits, site audits, or other strategies for ensuring accredited suppliers maintain adherence to the criteria described in paragraph (3).

children.label: iv
children.text: The ability of the organization to take into account the capacities of suppliers located in a rural area (as defined in ).section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: Whether the organization has established reasonable fees to be charged to suppliers applying for accreditation.

children.label: vi
children.text: Such other factors as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: B Designation
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall designate organizations to accredit suppliers furnishing the technical component of advanced diagnostic imaging services. The list of accreditation organizations so designated may be modified pursuant to subparagraph (C).January 1, 2010

children.heading: Review and modification of list of accreditation organizations
children.label: C Review and modification of list of accreditation organizations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall review the list of accreditation organizations designated under subparagraph (B) taking into account the factors under subparagraph (A). Taking into account the results of such review, the Secretary may, by regulation, modify the list of accreditation organizations designated under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Special rule for accreditations done prior to removal from list of designated accreditation organizations
children.label: ii Special rule for accreditations done prior to removal from list of designated accreditation organizations
children.text: In the case where the Secretary removes an organization from the list of accreditation organizations designated under subparagraph (B), any supplier that is accredited by the organization during the period beginning on the date on which the organization is designated as an accreditation organization under subparagraph (B) and ending on the date on which the organization is removed from such list shall be considered to have been accredited by an organization designated by the Secretary under subparagraph (B) for the remaining period such accreditation is in effect.

children.label: A
children.text: standards for qualifications of medical personnel who are not physicians and who furnish the technical component of advanced diagnostic imaging services;

children.label: B
children.text: standards for qualifications and responsibilities of medical directors and supervising physicians, including standards that recognize the considerations described in paragraph (4);

children.label: C
children.text: procedures to ensure that equipment used in furnishing the technical component of advanced diagnostic imaging services meets performance specifications;

children.label: D
children.text: standards that require the supplier have procedures in place to ensure the safety of persons who furnish the technical component of advanced diagnostic imaging services and individuals to whom such services are furnished;

children.label: E
children.text: standards that require the establishment and maintenance of a quality assurance and quality control program by the supplier that is adequate and appropriate to ensure the reliability, clarity, and accuracy of the technical quality of diagnostic images produced by such supplier; and

children.label: F
children.text: any other standards or procedures the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Recognition in standards for the evaluation of medical directors and supervising physicians
children.label: 4 Recognition in standards for the evaluation of medical directors and supervising physicians
A
children.text: in a particular specialty receives training in advanced diagnostic imaging services in a residency program;

children.label: B
children.text: has attained, through experience, the necessary expertise to be a medical director or a supervising physician;

children.label: C
children.text: has completed any continuing medical education courses relating to such services; or

children.label: D
children.text: has met such other standards as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Rule for accreditations made prior to designation
children.label: 5 Rule for accreditations made prior to designation
children.text: In the case of a supplier that is accredited before , by an accreditation organization designated by the Secretary under paragraph (2)(B) as of , such supplier shall be considered to have been accredited by an organization designated by the Secretary under such paragraph as of , for the remaining period such accreditation is in effect.January 1, 2010January 1, 2010January 1, 2012

children.heading: Reduction in payments for physician pathology services during 1991
children.label: f Reduction in payments for physician pathology services during 1991

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For physician pathology services furnished under this part during 1991, the prevailing charges used in a locality under this part shall be 7 percent below the prevailing charges used in the locality under this part in 1990 after March 31.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The prevailing charge for the technical and professional components of an physician pathology service furnished by a physician through an independent laboratory shall not be reduced pursuant to paragraph (1) to the extent that such reduction would reduce such prevailing charge below 115 percent of the prevailing charge for the professional component of such service when furnished by a hospital-based physician in the same locality. For purposes of the preceding sentence, an independent laboratory is a laboratory that is independent of a hospital and separate from the attending or consulting physicians’ office.3So in original. Probably should be “a”.3

children.heading: Payment for outpatient critical access hospital services
children.label: g Payment for outpatient critical access hospital services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of payment for outpatient critical access hospital services of a critical access hospital is equal to 101 percent of the reasonable costs of the hospital in providing such services, unless the hospital makes the election under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Election of cost-based hospital outpatient service payment plus fee schedule for professional services
children.label: 2 Election of cost-based hospital outpatient service payment plus fee schedule for professional services

children.heading: Facility fee
children.label: A Facility fee
children.text: With respect to facility services, not including any services for which payment may be made under subparagraph (B), 101 percent of the reasonable costs of the critical access hospital in providing such services.

children.heading: Fee schedule for professional services
children.label: B Fee schedule for professional services
children.text: With respect to professional services otherwise included within outpatient critical access hospital services, 115 percent of such amounts as would otherwise be paid under this part if such services were not included in outpatient critical access hospital services. Subsections (x) and (y) of section 1395 of this title shall not be taken into account in determining the amounts that would otherwise be paid pursuant to the preceding sentence.l

children.heading: Disregarding charges
children.label: 3 Disregarding charges
children.text: The payment amounts under this subsection shall be determined without regard to the amount of the customary or other charge.

children.heading: Treatment of clinical diagnostic laboratory services
children.label: 4 Treatment of clinical diagnostic laboratory services
children.text: No coinsurance, deductible, copayment, or other cost-sharing otherwise applicable under this part shall apply with respect to clinical diagnostic laboratory services furnished as an outpatient critical access hospital service. Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed as providing for payment for clinical diagnostic laboratory services furnished as part of outpatient critical access hospital services, other than on the basis described in this subsection. For purposes of the preceding sentence and , clinical diagnostic laboratory services furnished by a critical access hospital shall be treated as being furnished as part of outpatient critical access services without regard to whether the individual with respect to whom such services are furnished is physically present in the critical access hospital, or in a skilled nursing facility or a clinic (including a rural health clinic) that is operated by a critical access hospital, at the time the specimen is collected.section 1395x(mm)(3) of this title

children.heading: Coverage of costs for certain emergency room on-call providers
children.label: 5 Coverage of costs for certain emergency room on-call providers
children.text: In determining the reasonable costs of outpatient critical access hospital services under paragraphs (1) and (2)(A), the Secretary shall recognize as allowable costs, amounts (as defined by the Secretary) for reasonable compensation and related costs for physicians, physician assistants, nurse practitioners, and clinical nurse specialists who are on-call (as defined by the Secretary) to provide emergency services but who are not present on the premises of the critical access hospital involved, and are not otherwise furnishing services covered under this subchapter and are not on-call at any other provider or facility.

children.heading: Payment for prosthetic devices and orthotics and prosthetics
children.label: h Payment for prosthetic devices and orthotics and prosthetics

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Payment under this subsection for prosthetic devices and orthotics and prosthetics shall be made in a lump-sum amount for the purchase of the item in an amount equal to 80 percent of the payment basis described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: the actual charge for the item; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount recognized under paragraph (2) as the purchase price for the item.

children.heading: Exception for certain public home health agencies
children.label: C Exception for certain public home health agencies
children.text: Subparagraph (B)(i) shall not apply to an item furnished by a public home health agency (or by another home health agency which demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that a significant portion of its patients are low income) free of charge or at nominal charges to the public.

children.heading: Exclusive payment rule
children.label: D Exclusive payment rule
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (H)(ii), this subsection shall constitute the exclusive provision of this subchapter for payment for prosthetic devices, orthotics, and prosthetics under this part or under part A to a home health agency.

children.heading: Exception for certain items
children.label: E Exception for certain items
children.text: Payment for ostomy supplies, tracheostomy supplies, and urologicals shall be made in accordance with subparagraphs (B) and (C) of subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Special payment rules for certain prosthetics and custom-fabricated orthotics
children.label: F Special payment rules for certain prosthetics and custom-fabricated orthotics
I
children.text: furnished by a qualified practitioner; and

children.label: II
children.text: fabricated by a qualified practitioner or a qualified supplier at a facility that meets such criteria as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: An item described in this clause is an item of custom-fabricated orthotics that requires education, training, and experience to custom-fabricate and that is included in a list established by the Secretary in subclause (II). Such an item does not include shoes and shoe inserts.

children.heading: List of items
children.label: II List of items
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with appropriate experts in orthotics (including national organizations representing manufacturers of orthotics), shall establish and update as appropriate a list of items to which this subparagraph applies. No item may be included in such list unless the item is individually fabricated for the patient over a positive model of the patient.

children.label: I
children.text: is a qualified physical therapist or a qualified occupational therapist;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a State that provides for the licensing of orthotics and prosthetics, is licensed in orthotics or prosthetics by the State in which the item is supplied; or

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a State that does not provide for the licensing of orthotics and prosthetics, is specifically trained and educated to provide or manage the provision of prosthetics and custom-designed or -fabricated orthotics, and is certified by the American Board for Certification in Orthotics and Prosthetics, Inc. or by the Board for Orthotist/Prosthetist Certification, or is credentialed and approved by a program that the Secretary determines, in consultation with appropriate experts in orthotics and prosthetics, has training and education standards that are necessary to provide such prosthetics and orthotics.

children.heading: Qualified supplier defined
children.label: iv Qualified supplier defined
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “qualified supplier” means any entity that is accredited by the American Board for Certification in Orthotics and Prosthetics, Inc. or by the Board for Orthotist/Prosthetist Certification, or accredited and approved by a program that the Secretary determines has accreditation and approval standards that are essentially equivalent to those of such Board.

children.heading: Replacement of prosthetic devices and parts
children.label: G Replacement of prosthetic devices and parts
I
children.text: A change in the physiological condition of the patient.

children.label: II
children.text: An irreparable change in the condition of the device, or in a part of the device.

children.label: III
children.text: The condition of the device, or the part of the device, requires repairs and the cost of such repairs would be more than 60 percent of the cost of a replacement device, or, as the case may be, of the part being replaced.

children.heading: Confirmation may be required if device or part being replaced is less than 3 years old
children.label: ii Confirmation may be required if device or part being replaced is less than 3 years old
I
children.text: such determination shall be controlling; and

children.label: II
children.text: such replacement device or part shall be deemed to be reasonable and necessary for purposes of ;section 1395y(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Application of competitive acquisition to orthotics; limitation of inherent reasonableness authority
children.label: H Application of competitive acquisition to orthotics; limitation of inherent reasonableness authority
i
children.text: the payment basis under this subsection for such orthotics furnished in such area shall be the payment basis determined under such competitive acquisition program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: subject to subsection (a)(1)(G), the Secretary may use information on the payment determined under such competitive acquisition programs to adjust the payment amount otherwise recognized under subparagraph (B)(ii) for an area that is not a competitive acquisition area under , and in the case of such adjustment, paragraphs (8) and (9) of shall not be applied.section 1395w–3 of this titlesection 1395u(b) of this title

children.heading: Computation of local purchase price
children.label: A Computation of local purchase price
i
children.text: The carrier shall compute a base local purchase price for each item equal to the average reasonable charge in the locality for the purchase of the item for the 12-month period ending with June 1987.

children.label: I
children.text: in 1989 and 1990, equal to the base local purchase price computed under clause (i) increased by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 6-month period ending with December 1987, or

children.label: II
children.text: in 1991, 1992 or 1993, equal to the local purchase price computed under this clause for the previous year increased by the applicable percentage increase for the year.

children.heading: Computation of regional purchase price
children.label: B Computation of regional purchase price
i
children.text: for 1992, equal to the average (weighted by relative volume of all claims among carriers) of the local purchase prices for the carriers in the region computed under subparagraph (A)(ii)(II) for the year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: for each subsequent year, equal to the regional purchase price computed under this subparagraph for the previous year increased by the applicable percentage increase for the year.

children.label: i
children.text: in 1989, 1990, or 1991, is 100 percent of the local purchase price computed under subparagraph (A)(ii);

children.label: ii
children.text: in 1992, is the sum of (I) 75 percent of the local purchase price computed under subparagraph (A)(ii)(II) for 1992, and (II) 25 percent of the regional purchase price computed under subparagraph (B) for 1992;

children.label: iii
children.text: in 1993, is the sum of (I) 50 percent of the local purchase price computed under subparagraph (A)(ii)(II) for 1993, and (II) 50 percent of the regional purchase price computed under subparagraph (B) for 1993; and

children.label: iv
children.text: in 1994 or a subsequent year, is the regional purchase price computed under subparagraph (B) for that year.

children.label: i
children.text: in 1992, may not exceed 125 percent, and may not be lower than 85 percent, of the average of the purchase prices recognized under such subparagraph for all the carrier service areas in the United States in that year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in a subsequent year, may not exceed 120 percent, and may not be lower than 90 percent, of the average of the purchase prices recognized under such subparagraph for all the carrier service areas in the United States in that year.

children.heading: Applicability of certain provisions relating to durable medical equipment
children.label: 3 Applicability of certain provisions relating to durable medical equipment
children.text: Paragraphs (12), (15), and (17) and subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (10) and paragraph (11) of subsection (a) shall apply to prosthetic devices, orthotics, and prosthetics in the same manner as such provisions apply to covered items under such subsection, and paragraph (23) of subsection (a) shall apply to prosthetic devices, orthotics, and prosthetics in the same manner as such provision applies to items for which payment may be made under such subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: for 1991, 0 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: for 1992 and 1993, the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year;

children.label: iii
children.text: for 1994 and 1995, 0 percent;

children.label: iv
children.text: for 1996 and 1997, the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year;

children.label: v
children.text: for each of the years 1998 through 2000, 1 percent;

children.label: vi
children.text: for 2001, the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (U.S. city average) for the 12-month period ending with June 2000;

children.label: vii
children.text: for 2002, 1 percent;

children.label: viii
children.text: for 2003, the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year;

children.label: ix
children.text: for 2004, 2005, and 2006, 0 percent;

children.label: x
children.text: for for each of 2007 through 2010, the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year; and4So in original.4

children.label: I
children.text: the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year, reduced by—

children.label: II
children.text: the productivity adjustment described in .section 1395ww(b)(3)(B)(xi)(II) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the term “prosthetic devices” has the meaning given such term in , except that such term does not include parenteral and enteral nutrition nutrients, supplies, and equipment and does not include an implantable item for which payment may be made under section 1395(t) of this title; andsection 1395x(s)(8) of this titlel

children.label: C
children.text: the term “orthotics and prosthetics” has the meaning given such term in (and includes shoes described in ), but does not include intraocular lenses or medical supplies (including catheters, catheter supplies, ostomy bags, and supplies related to ostomy care) furnished by a home health agency under .section 1395x(s)(9) of this titlesection 1395x(s)(12) of this titlesection 1395x(m)(5) of this title

children.heading: Documentation created by orthotists and prosthetists
children.label: 5 Documentation created by orthotists and prosthetists
children.text: For purposes of determining the reasonableness and medical necessity of orthotics and prosthetics, documentation created by an orthotist or prosthetist shall be considered part of the individual’s medical record to support documentation created by eligible professionals described in .section 1395w–4(k)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the actual charge for the item; or

children.label: B
children.text: a payment amount determined in accordance with the methodology described in subparagraphs (B) and (C) of subsection (a)(2) (except that in applying such methodology, the national limited payment amount referred to in such subparagraphs shall be initially computed based on local payment amounts using average reasonable charges for the 12-month period ending , increased by the covered item updates described in such subsection for 1993 and 1994).December 31, 1992

children.label: A
children.text: furnished as an incident to a physician’s professional service; or

children.label: B
children.text: furnished by a home health agency.

children.heading: Requirements for suppliers of medical equipment and supplies
children.label: j Requirements for suppliers of medical equipment and supplies

children.heading: Issuance and renewal of supplier number
children.label: 1 Issuance and renewal of supplier number

children.heading: Payment
children.label: A Payment
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), no payment may be made under this part after , for items furnished by a supplier of medical equipment and supplies unless such supplier obtains (and renews at such intervals as the Secretary may require) a supplier number.October 31, 1994

children.heading: Standards for possessing a supplier number
children.label: B Standards for possessing a supplier number
i
children.text: for medical equipment and supplies furnished on or after , and before , the supplier meets standards prescribed by the Secretary in regulations issued on ; andOctober 31, 1994January 1, 1996June 18, 1992

children.label: I
children.text: comply with all applicable State and Federal licensure and regulatory requirements;

children.label: II
children.text: maintain a physical facility on an appropriate site;

children.label: III
children.text: have proof of appropriate liability insurance; and

children.label: IV
children.text: meet such other requirements as the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: Exception for items furnished as incident to a physician’s service
children.label: C Exception for items furnished as incident to a physician’s service
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to medical equipment and supplies furnished incident to a physician’s service.

children.heading: Prohibition against multiple supplier numbers
children.label: D Prohibition against multiple supplier numbers
children.text: The Secretary may not issue more than one supplier number to any supplier of medical equipment and supplies unless the issuance of more than one number is appropriate to identify subsidiary or regional entities under the supplier’s ownership or control.

children.heading: Prohibition against delegation of supplier determinations
children.label: E Prohibition against delegation of supplier determinations
children.text: The Secretary may not delegate (other than by contract under ) the responsibility to determine whether suppliers meet the standards necessary to obtain a supplier number.section 1395u of this title

children.heading: Limitation on information provided by suppliers on certificates of medical necessity
children.label: A Limitation on information provided by suppliers on certificates of medical necessity
I
children.text: An identification of the supplier and the beneficiary to whom such medical equipment and supplies are furnished.

children.label: II
children.text: A description of such medical equipment and supplies.

children.label: III
children.text: Any product code identifying such medical equipment and supplies.

children.label: IV
children.text: Any other administrative information (other than information relating to the beneficiary’s medical condition) identified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Information on payment amount and charges
children.label: ii Information on payment amount and charges
children.text: If a supplier distributes a certificate of medical necessity containing any of the information permitted to be supplied under clause (i), the supplier shall also list on the certificate of medical necessity the fee schedule amount and the supplier’s charge for the medical equipment or supplies being furnished prior to distribution of such certificate to the physician.

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: iii Penalty
children.text: Any supplier of medical equipment and supplies who knowingly and willfully distributes a certificate of medical necessity in violation of clause (i) or fails to provide the information required under clause (ii) is subject to a civil money penalty in an amount not to exceed $1,000 for each such certificate of medical necessity so distributed. The provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to civil money penalties under this subparagraph in the same manner as they apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: “Certificate of medical necessity” defined
children.label: B “Certificate of medical necessity” defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “certificate of medical necessity” means a form or other document containing information required by the carrier to be submitted to show that an item is reasonable and necessary for the diagnosis or treatment of illness or injury or to improve the functioning of a malformed body member.

children.heading: Coverage and review criteria
children.label: 3 Coverage and review criteria
children.text: The Secretary shall annually review the coverage and utilization of items of medical equipment and supplies to determine whether such items should be made subject to coverage and utilization review criteria, and if appropriate, shall develop and apply such criteria to such items.

children.label: A
children.text: furnishes an item or service to a beneficiary for which no payment may be made by reason of paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: furnishes an item or service to a beneficiary for which payment is denied in advance under subsection (a)(15); or

children.label: C
children.text: furnishes an item or service to a beneficiary for which payment is denied under ;section 1395y(a)(1) of this title

children.text: durable medical equipment (as defined in );section 1395x(n) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: prosthetic devices (as described in );section 1395x(s)(8) of this title

children.label: C

children.label: E

children.label: F

children.label: G
children.text: such other items as the Secretary may determine; and5So in original. Two subpars. (G) have been enacted.5
