children.text: self-administered erythropoetin (as described in ).section 1395x(s)(2)(P) of this title

children.heading: Payment for outpatient therapy services and comprehensive outpatient rehabilitation services
children.label: k Payment for outpatient therapy services and comprehensive outpatient rehabilitation services
A
children.text: for services furnished during 1998, the amount determined under paragraph (2); or

children.label: i
children.text: the actual charge for the services, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the applicable fee schedule amount (as defined in paragraph (3)) for the services.

children.heading: Payment in 1998 based upon adjusted reasonable costs
children.label: 2 Payment in 1998 based upon adjusted reasonable costs
A
children.text: the charges imposed for the services, or

children.label: B
children.text: the adjusted reasonable costs (as defined in paragraph (4)) for the services,

children.heading: Applicable fee schedule amount
children.label: 3 Applicable fee schedule amount
children.text: In this subsection, the term “applicable fee schedule amount” means, with respect to services furnished in a year, the amount determined under the fee schedule established under for such services furnished during the year or, if there is no such fee schedule established for such services, the amount determined under the fee schedule established for such comparable services as the Secretary specifies.section 1395w–4 of this title

children.heading: Adjusted reasonable costs
children.label: 4 Adjusted reasonable costs
children.text: In paragraph (2), the term “adjusted reasonable costs” means, with respect to any services, reasonable costs determined for such services, reduced by 10 percent. The 10-percent reduction shall not apply to services described in section 1395(a)(8)(B) of this title (relating to services provided by hospitals).l

children.heading: Uniform coding
children.label: 5 Uniform coding
children.text: For claims for services submitted on or after , for which the amount of payment is determined under this subsection, the claim shall include a code (or codes) under a uniform coding system specified by the Secretary that identifies the services furnished.April 1, 1998

children.heading: Restraint on billing
children.label: 6 Restraint on billing
children.text: The provisions of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of shall apply to therapy services for which payment is made under this subsection in the same manner as they apply to services provided by a practitioner described in .section 1395u(b)(18) of this titlesection 1395u(b)(18)(C) of this title

children.heading: Adjustment in discount for certain multiple therapy services
children.label: 7 Adjustment in discount for certain multiple therapy services
children.text: In the case of therapy services furnished on or after , and for which payment is made under this subsection pursuant to the applicable fee schedule amount (as defined in paragraph (3)), instead of the 25 percent multiple procedure payment reduction specified in the final rule published by the Secretary in the Federal Register on , the reduction percentage shall be 50 percent.April 1, 2013November 29, 2010

children.heading: Establishment of fee schedule for ambulance services
children.label: l Establishment of fee schedule for ambulance services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a fee schedule for payment for ambulance services whether provided directly by a supplier or provider or under arrangement with a provider under this part through a negotiated rulemaking process described in title 5 and in accordance with the requirements of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: establish mechanisms to control increases in expenditures for ambulance services under this part;

children.label: B
children.text: establish definitions for ambulance services which link payments to the type of services provided;

children.label: C
children.text: consider appropriate regional and operational differences;

children.label: D
children.text: consider adjustments to payment rates to account for inflation and other relevant factors; and

children.label: E
children.text: phase in the application of the payment rates under the fee schedule in an efficient and fair manner consistent with paragraph (11), except that such phase-in shall provide for full payment of any national mileage rate for ambulance services provided by suppliers that are paid by carriers in any of the 50 States where payment by a carrier for such services for all such suppliers in such State did not, prior to the implementation of the fee schedule, include a separate amount for all mileage within the county from which the beneficiary is transported.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that the aggregate amount of payments made for ambulance services under this part during 2000 does not exceed the aggregate amount of payments which would have been made for such services under this part during such year if the amendments made by section 4531(a) of the Balanced Budget Act of 1997 continued in effect, except that in making such determination the Secretary shall assume an update in such payments for 2002 equal to percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (U.S. city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year reduced in the case of 2002 by 1.0 percentage points;

children.label: B
children.text: set the payment amounts provided under the fee schedule for services furnished in 2001 and each subsequent year at amounts equal to the payment amounts under the fee schedule for services furnished during the previous year, increased, subject to subparagraph (C) and the succeeding sentence of this paragraph, by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (U.S. city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year reduced in the case of 2002 by 1.0 percentage points; and

children.label: C
children.text: for 2011 and each subsequent year, after determining the percentage increase under subparagraph (B) for the year, reduce such percentage increase by the productivity adjustment described in .section 1395ww(b)(3)(B)(xi)(II) of this title

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 4 Consultation
children.text: In establishing the fee schedule for ambulance services under this subsection, the Secretary shall consult with various national organizations representing individuals and entities who furnish and regulate ambulance services and share with such organizations relevant data in establishing such schedule.

children.heading: Limitation on review
children.label: 5 Limitation on review
children.text: There shall be no administrative or judicial review under or otherwise of the amounts established under the fee schedule for ambulance services under this subsection, including matters described in paragraph (2).section 1395ff of this title

children.heading: Restraint on billing
children.label: 6 Restraint on billing
children.text: The provisions of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of shall apply to ambulance services for which payment is made under this subsection in the same manner as they apply to services provided by a practitioner described in .section 1395u(b)(18) of this titlesection 1395u(b)(18)(C) of this title

children.heading: Coding system
children.label: 7 Coding system
children.text: The Secretary may require the claim for any services for which the amount of payment is determined under this subsection to include a code (or codes) under a uniform coding system specified by the Secretary that identifies the services furnished.

children.heading: Services furnished by critical access hospitals
children.label: 8 Services furnished by critical access hospitals
A
children.text: by a critical access hospital (as defined in ), orsection 1395x(mm)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: by an entity that is owned and operated by a critical access hospital,

children.heading: Transitional assistance for rural providers
children.label: 9 Transitional assistance for rural providers
children.text: In the case of ground ambulance services furnished on or after , and before , for which the transportation originates in a rural area (as defined in ) or in a rural census tract of a metropolitan statistical area (as determined under the most recent modification of the Goldsmith Modification, originally published in the Federal Register on (57 Fed. Reg. 6725)), the fee schedule established under this subsection shall provide that, with respect to the payment rate for mileage for a trip above 17 miles, and up to 50 miles, the rate otherwise established shall be increased by not less than ½ of the additional payment per mile established for the first 17 miles of such a trip originating in a rural area.July 1, 2001January 1, 2004February 27, 1992section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this title

children.heading: Phase-in providing floor using blend of fee schedule and regional fee schedules
children.label: 10 Phase-in providing floor using blend of fee schedule and regional fee schedules
A
children.text: For 2004 (for services furnished on or after ), the blended rate shall be based 20 percent on the fee schedule under paragraph (1) and 80 percent on the regional fee schedule.July 1, 2004

children.label: B
children.text: For 2005, the blended rate shall be based 40 percent on the fee schedule under paragraph (1) and 60 percent on the regional fee schedule.

children.label: C
children.text: For 2006, the blended rate shall be based 60 percent on the fee schedule under paragraph (1) and 40 percent on the regional fee schedule.

children.label: D
children.text: For 2007, 2008, and 2009, the blended rate shall be based 80 percent on the fee schedule under paragraph (1) and 20 percent on the regional fee schedule.

children.label: E
children.text: For 2010 and each succeeding year, the blended rate shall be based 100 percent on the fee schedule under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Adjustment in payment for certain long trips
children.label: 11 Adjustment in payment for certain long trips
children.text: In the case of ground ambulance services furnished on or after , and before , regardless of where the transportation originates, the fee schedule established under this subsection shall provide that, with respect to the payment rate for mileage for a trip above 50 miles the per mile rate otherwise established shall be increased by ¼ of the payment per mile otherwise applicable to miles in excess of 50 miles in such trip.July 1, 2004January 1, 2009

children.heading: Assistance for rural providers furnishing services in low population density areas
children.label: 12 Assistance for rural providers furnishing services in low population density areas

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of ground ambulance services furnished on or after , and before , for which the transportation originates in a qualified rural area (identified under subparagraph (B)(iii)), the Secretary shall provide for a percent increase in the base rate of the fee schedule for a trip established under this subsection. In establishing such percent increase, the Secretary shall estimate the average cost per trip for such services (not taking into account mileage) in the lowest quartile as compared to the average cost per trip for such services (not taking into account mileage) in the highest quartile of all rural county populations.July 1, 2004January 1, 2028

children.heading: Identification of qualified rural areas
children.label: B Identification of qualified rural areas

children.heading: Determination of population density in area
children.label: i Determination of population density in area
children.text: Based upon data from the United States decennial census for the year 2000, the Secretary shall determine, for each rural area, the population density for that area.

children.heading: Ranking of areas
children.label: ii Ranking of areas
children.text: The Secretary shall rank each such area based on such population density.

children.heading: Identification of qualified rural areas
children.label: iii Identification of qualified rural areas
children.text: The Secretary shall identify those areas (in subparagraph (A) referred to as “qualified rural areas”) with the lowest population densities that represent, if each such area were weighted by the population of such area (as used in computing such population densities), an aggregate total of 25 percent of the total of the population of all such areas.

children.heading: Rural area
children.label: iv Rural area
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “rural area” has the meaning given such term in . If feasible, the Secretary shall treat a rural census tract of a metropolitan statistical area (as determined under the most recent modification of the Goldsmith Modification, originally published in the Federal Register on (57 Fed. Reg. 6725) as a rural area for purposes of this paragraph.section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this titleFebruary 27, 1992

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: v Judicial review
children.text: There shall be no administrative or judicial review under section 1395ff, 1395 of this title, or otherwise, respecting the identification of an area under this subparagraph.oo

children.heading: Temporary increase for ground ambulance services
children.label: 13 Temporary increase for ground ambulance services
i
children.text: a rural area described in paragraph (9) or in a rural census tract described in such paragraph, the fee schedule established under this section shall provide that the rate for the service otherwise established, after the application of any increase under paragraphs (11) and (12), shall be increased by 2 percent (or 3 percent if such service is furnished on or after , and before ); andJuly 1, 2008January 1, 2028

children.label: ii
children.text: an area not described in clause (i), the fee schedule established under this subsection shall provide that the rate for the service otherwise established, after the application of any increase under paragraph (11), shall be increased by 1 percent (or 2 percent if such service is furnished on or after , and before ).July 1, 2008January 1, 2028

children.heading: Application of increased payments after applicable period
children.label: B Application of increased payments after applicable period
children.text: The increased payments under subparagraph (A) shall not be taken into account in calculating payments for services furnished after the applicable period specified in such subparagraph.

children.heading: Providing appropriate coverage of rural air ambulance services
children.label: 14 Providing appropriate coverage of rural air ambulance services
i
children.text: is reasonable and necessary based on the health condition of the individual being transported at or immediately prior to the time of the transport; and

children.label: ii
children.text: complies with equipment and crew requirements established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Satisfaction of requirement of medically necessary
children.label: B Satisfaction of requirement of medically necessary
i
children.text: subject to subparagraph (D), such service is requested by a physician or other qualified medical personnel (as specified by the Secretary) who certifies or reasonably determines that the individual’s condition is such that the time needed to transport the individual by land or the instability of transportation by land poses a threat to the individual’s survival or seriously endangers the individual’s health; or

children.label: ii
children.text: such service is furnished pursuant to a protocol that is established by a State or regional emergency medical service (EMS) agency and recognized or approved by the Secretary under which the use of an air ambulance is recommended, if such agency does not have an ownership interest in the entity furnishing such service.

children.heading: Rural air ambulance service defined
children.label: C Rural air ambulance service defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “rural air ambulance service” means fixed wing and rotary wing air ambulance service in which the point of pick up of the individual occurs in a rural area (as defined in ) or in a rural census tract of a metropolitan statistical area (as determined under the most recent modification of the Goldsmith Modification, originally published in the Federal Register on (57 Fed. Reg. 6725)).section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this titleFebruary 27, 1992

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subparagraph (B)(i) shall not apply if there is a financial or employment relationship between the person requesting the rural air ambulance service and the entity furnishing the ambulance service, or an entity under common ownership with the entity furnishing the air ambulance service, or a financial relationship between an immediate family member of such requester and such an entity.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Where a hospital and the entity furnishing rural air ambulance services are under common ownership, clause (i) shall not apply to remuneration (through employment or other relationship) by the hospital of the requester or immediate family member if the remuneration is for provider-based physician services furnished in a hospital (as described in ) which are reimbursed under part A and the amount of the remuneration is unrelated directly or indirectly to the provision of rural air ambulance services.section 1395xx of this title

children.heading: Payment adjustment for non-emergency ambulance transports for ESRD beneficiaries
children.label: 15 Payment adjustment for non-emergency ambulance transports for ESRD beneficiaries
children.text: The fee schedule amount otherwise applicable under the preceding provisions of this subsection shall be reduced by 10 percent for ambulance services furnished during the period beginning on , and ending on , and by 23 percent for such services furnished on or after , consisting of non-emergency basic life support services involving transport of an individual with end-stage renal disease for renal dialysis services (as described in ) furnished other than on an emergency basis by a provider of services or a renal dialysis facility.October 1, 2013September 30, 2018October 1, 2018section 1395rr(b)(14)(B) of this title

children.heading: Prior authorization for repetitive scheduled non-emergent ambulance transports
children.label: 16 Prior authorization for repetitive scheduled non-emergent ambulance transports

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning , if the expansion to all States of the model of prior authorization described in paragraph (2) of section 515(a) of the Medicare Access and CHIP Reauthorization Act of 2015 meets the requirements described in paragraphs (1) through (3) of , then the Secretary shall expand such model to all States.January 1, 2017section 1315a(c) of this title

children.heading: Funding
children.label: B Funding
children.text: The Secretary shall use funds made available under to carry out this paragraph.section 1395ddd(h)(10) of this title

children.heading: Clarification regarding budget neutrality
children.label: C Clarification regarding budget neutrality
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph may be construed to limit or modify the application of to models described in such section, including with respect to the model described in subparagraph (A) and expanded beginning on , under such subparagraph.section 1315a(b)(3)(B) of this titleJanuary 1, 2017

children.heading: Submission of cost and other information
children.label: 17 Submission of cost and other information

children.heading: Development of data collection system
children.label: A Development of data collection system
i
children.text: needed to evaluate the extent to which reported costs relate to payment rates under this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: on the utilization of capital equipment and ambulance capacity, including information consistent with the type of information described in ; andsection 1320a(a) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: on different types of ground ambulance services furnished in different geographic locations, including rural areas and low population density areas described in paragraph (12).

children.heading: Specification of data collection system
children.label: B Specification of data collection system
I
children.text: not later than , specify the data collection system under subparagraph (A); andDecember 31, 2019

children.label: II
children.text: identify the providers and suppliers of ground ambulance services that would be required to submit information under such data collection system, including the representative sample described in clause (ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Not later than , with respect to the data collection for the first year under such system, and for each subsequent year through 2024, the Secretary shall determine a representative sample to submit information under the data collection system.December 31, 2019

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: II Requirements
children.text: The sample under subclause (I) shall be representative of the different types of providers and suppliers of ground ambulance services (such as those providers and suppliers that are part of an emergency service or part of a government organization) and the geographic locations in which ground ambulance services are furnished (such as urban, rural, and low population density areas).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: III Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not include an individual provider or supplier of ground ambulance services in the sample under subclause (I) in 2 consecutive years, to the extent practicable.

children.heading: Reporting of cost information
children.label: C Reporting of cost information
children.text: For each year, a provider or supplier of ground ambulance services identified by the Secretary under subparagraph (B)(i)(II) as being required to submit information under the data collection system with respect to a period for the year shall submit to the Secretary information specified under the system. Such information shall be submitted in a form and manner, and at a time, specified by the Secretary for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Payment reduction for failure to report
children.label: D Payment reduction for failure to report
I
children.text: is required to submit information under the data collection system with respect to a period under subparagraph (C); and

children.label: II
children.text: does not sufficiently submit such information, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Applicable period defined
children.label: ii Applicable period defined
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “applicable period” means, with respect to a provider or supplier of ground ambulance services, a year specified by the Secretary not more than 2 years after the end of the period with respect to which the Secretary has made a determination under clause (i)(II) that the provider or supplier of ground ambulance services failed to sufficiently submit information under the data collection system.

children.heading: Hardship exemption
children.label: iii Hardship exemption
children.text: The Secretary may exempt a provider or supplier from the payment reduction under clause (i) with respect to an applicable period in the event of significant hardship, such as a natural disaster, bankruptcy, or other similar situation that the Secretary determines interfered with the ability of the provider or supplier of ground ambulance services to submit such information in a timely manner for the specified period.

children.heading: Informal review
children.label: iv Informal review
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a process under which a provider or supplier of ground ambulance services may seek an informal review of a determination that the provider or supplier is subject to the payment reduction under clause (i).

children.heading: Revision of data collection system
children.label: i Revision of data collection system
children.text: The Secretary may, as the Secretary determines appropriate and, if available, taking into consideration the report (or reports) under subparagraph (F), revise the data collection system under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Subsequent data collection
children.label: ii Subsequent data collection
children.text: In order to continue to evaluate the extent to which reported costs relate to payment rates under this subsection and for other purposes the Secretary deems appropriate, the Secretary shall require providers and suppliers of ground ambulance services to submit information for years after 2024 as the Secretary determines appropriate, but in no case less often than once every 3 years.

children.heading: Ground ambulance data collection system study
children.label: F Ground ambulance data collection system study

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than the second June 15th following the date on which the Secretary transmits data for the first representative sample of providers and suppliers of ground ambulance services to the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission, and as determined necessary by such Commission thereafter,, such Commission shall assess, and submit to Congress a report on, information submitted by providers and suppliers of ground ambulance services through the data collection system under subparagraph (A), the adequacy of payments for ground ambulance services under this subsection, and geographic variations in the cost of furnishing such services.4

children.label: I
children.text: An analysis of information submitted through the data collection system.

children.label: II
children.text: An analysis of any burden on providers and suppliers of ground ambulance services associated with the data collection system.

children.label: III
children.text: A recommendation as to whether information should continue to be submitted through such data collection system or if such system should be revised under subparagraph (E)(i).

children.label: IV
children.text: Other information determined appropriate by the Commission.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: G Public availability
children.text: The Secretary shall post information on the results of the data collection under this paragraph on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: H Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall implement this paragraph through notice and comment rulemaking.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: I Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to the collection of information required under this subsection.

children.heading: Limitations on review
children.label: J Limitations on review
children.text: There shall be no administrative or judicial review under , section 1395 of this title, or otherwise of the data collection system or identification of respondents under this paragraph.section 1395ff of this titleoo

children.heading: Funding for implementation
children.label: K Funding for implementation
children.text: For purposes of carrying out subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under , of $15,000,000 to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account for fiscal year 2018. Amounts transferred under this subparagraph shall remain available until expended.section 1395t of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (8) and (9), the Secretary shall pay for telehealth services that are furnished via a telecommunications system by a physician (as defined in ) or a practitioner (as defined in paragraph (4)(E)) to an eligible telehealth individual enrolled under this part notwithstanding that the individual physician or practitioner providing the telehealth service is not at the same location as the beneficiary. For purposes of the preceding sentence, in the case of any Federal telemedicine demonstration program conducted in Alaska or Hawaii, the term “telecommunications system” includes store-and-forward technologies that provide for the asynchronous transmission of health care information in single or multimedia formats.section 1395x(r) of this title

children.heading: Distant site
children.label: A Distant site
children.text: Subject to paragraph (8), the Secretary shall pay to a physician or practitioner located at a distant site that furnishes a telehealth service to an eligible telehealth individual an amount equal to the amount that such physician or practitioner would have been paid under this subchapter had such service been furnished without the use of a telecommunications system.

children.heading: Facility fee for originating site
children.label: B Facility fee for originating site
I
children.text: for the period beginning on , and ending on , and for 2002, $20; andOctober 1, 2001December 31, 2001

children.label: II
children.text: for a subsequent year, the facility fee specified in subclause (I) or this subclause for the preceding year increased by the percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in ) for such subsequent year.section 1395u(i)(3) of this title

children.heading: No facility fee if originating site is the home
children.label: ii No facility fee if originating site is the home
children.text: No facility fee shall be paid under this subparagraph to an originating site described in paragraph (4)(C)(ii)(X).

children.heading: No facility fee for new sites
children.label: iii No facility fee for new sites
children.text: In the case that the emergency period described in ends before , with respect to telehealth services identified in paragraph (4)(F)(i) as of , that are furnished during the period beginning on the first day after the end of such emergency period and ending , a facility fee shall only be paid under this subparagraph to an originating site that is described in paragraph (4)(C)(ii) (other than subclause (X) of such paragraph).section 1320b–5(g)(1)(B) of this titleDecember 31, 2024March 15, 2022December 31, 2027

children.heading: Telepresenter not required
children.label: C Telepresenter not required
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as requiring an eligible telehealth individual to be presented by a physician or practitioner at the originating site for the furnishing of a service via a telecommunications system, unless it is medically necessary (as determined by the physician or practitioner at the distant site).

children.heading: Physician and practitioner
children.label: A Physician and practitioner
children.text: The provisions of and subparagraphs (A) and (B) of shall apply to a physician or practitioner receiving payment under this subsection in the same manner as they apply to physicians or practitioners under such sections.section 1395w–4(g) of this titlesection 1395u(b)(18) of this title

children.heading: Originating site
children.label: B Originating site
children.text: The provisions of shall apply to originating sites receiving a facility fee in the same manner as they apply to practitioners under such section.section 1395u(b)(18) of this title

children.heading: Distant site
children.label: A Distant site
children.text: Subject to paragraph (8), the term “distant site” means the site at which the physician or practitioner is located at the time the service is provided via a telecommunications system.

children.heading: Eligible telehealth individual
children.label: B Eligible telehealth individual
children.text: The term “eligible telehealth individual” means an individual enrolled under this part who receives a telehealth service furnished at an originating site.

children.label: I
children.text: in an area that is designated as a rural health professional shortage area under ;section 254e(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: in a county that is not included in a Metropolitan Statistical Area; or

children.label: III
children.text: from an entity that participates in a Federal telemedicine demonstration project that has been approved by (or receives funding from) the Secretary of Health and Human Services as of .December 31, 2000

children.label: I
children.text: The office of a physician or practitioner.

children.label: II
children.text: A critical access hospital (as defined in ).section 1395x(mm)(1) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: A rural health clinic (as defined in ).section 1395x(aa)(2) of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: A Federally qualified health center (as defined in ).section 1395x(aa)(4) of this title

children.label: V
children.text: A hospital (as defined in ).section 1395x(e) of this title

children.label: VI
children.text: A hospital-based or critical access hospital-based renal dialysis center (including satellites).

children.label: VII
children.text: A skilled nursing facility (as defined in ).section 1395i–3(a) of this title

children.label: VIII
children.text: A community mental health center (as defined in ).section 1395x(ff)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: IX
children.text: A renal dialysis facility, but only for purposes of .section 1395rr(b)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: X
children.text: The home of an individual, but only for purposes of or telehealth services described in paragraph (7).section 1395rr(b)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: XI
children.text: A rural emergency hospital (as defined in ).section 1395x(kkk)(2) of this title

children.heading: Expanding access to telehealth services
children.label: iii Expanding access to telehealth services
children.text: In the case that the emergency period described in ends before , with respect to telehealth services identified in subparagraph (F)(i) as of , that are furnished during the period beginning on the first day after the end of such emergency period and ending on , the term “originating site” means any site in the United States at which the eligible telehealth individual is located at the time the service is furnished via a telecommunications system, including the home of an individual.section 1320b–5(g)(1)(B) of this titleDecember 31, 2024March 15, 2022December 31, 2027

children.heading: Physician
children.label: D Physician
children.text: The term “physician” has the meaning given that term in .section 1395x(r) of this title

children.heading: Practitioner
children.label: E Practitioner
children.text: The term “practitioner” has the meaning given that term in and, in the case that the emergency period described in ends before , for the period beginning on the first day after the end of such emergency period and ending on , shall include a qualified occupational therapist (as such term is used in ), a qualified physical therapist (as such term is used in ), a qualified speech-language pathologist (as defined in section 1395x()(4)(A) of this title), and a qualified audiologist (as defined in section 1395x()(4)(B) of this title).section 1395u(b)(18)(C) of this titlesection 1320b–5(g)(1)(B) of this titlesection 1395x(g) of this titlesection 1395x(p) of this titleDecember 31, 2024December 31, 2027llll

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (8), the term “telehealth service” means professional consultations, office visits, and office psychiatry services (identified as of , by HCPCS codes 99241–99275, 99201–99215, 90804–90809, and 90862 (and as subsequently modified by the Secretary)), and any additional service specified by the Secretary.July 1, 2000

children.heading: Yearly update
children.label: ii Yearly update
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a process that provides, on an annual basis, for the addition or deletion of services (and HCPCS codes), as appropriate, to those specified in clause (i) for authorized payment under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Treatment of home dialysis monthly ESRD-related visit
children.label: 5 Treatment of home dialysis monthly ESRD-related visit
children.text: The geographic requirements described in paragraph (4)(C)(i) shall not apply with respect to telehealth services furnished on or after , for purposes of , at an originating site described in subclause (VI), (IX), or (X) of paragraph (4)(C)(ii).January 1, 2019section 1395rr(b)(3)(B) of this title

children.heading: Non-application of originating site requirements
children.label: A Non-application of originating site requirements
children.text: The requirements described in paragraph (4)(C) shall not apply with respect to telehealth services furnished on or after , for purposes of diagnosis, evaluation, or treatment of symptoms of an acute stroke, as determined by the Secretary.January 1, 2019

children.heading: Inclusion of certain sites
children.label: B Inclusion of certain sites
children.text: With respect to telehealth services described in subparagraph (A), the term “originating site” shall include any hospital (as defined in ) or critical access hospital (as defined in ), any mobile stroke unit (as defined by the Secretary), or any other site determined appropriate by the Secretary, at which the eligible telehealth individual is located at the time the service is furnished via a telecommunications system.section 1395x(e) of this titlesection 1395x(mm)(1) of this title

children.heading: No originating site facility fee for new sites
children.label: C No originating site facility fee for new sites
children.text: No facility fee shall be paid under paragraph (2)(B) to an originating site with respect to a telehealth service described in subparagraph (A) if the originating site does not otherwise meet the requirements for an originating site under paragraph (4)(C).

children.heading: Treatment of substance use disorder services and mental health services furnished through telehealth
children.label: 7 Treatment of substance use disorder services and mental health services furnished through telehealth

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The geographic requirements described in paragraph (4)(C)(i) shall not apply with respect to telehealth services furnished on or after , to an eligible telehealth individual with a substance use disorder diagnosis for purposes of treatment of such disorder or co-occurring mental health disorder, as determined by the Secretary, or, on or after the first day after the end of the emergency period described in , subject to subparagraph (B), to an eligible telehealth individual for purposes of diagnosis, evaluation, or treatment of a mental health disorder, as determined by the Secretary, at an originating site described in paragraph (4)(C)(ii) (other than an originating site described in subclause (IX) of such paragraph) or, for the period for which clause (iii) of paragraph (4)(C) applies, at any site described in such clause.July 1, 2019section 1320b–5(g)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Requirements for mental health services furnished through telehealth
children.label: B Requirements for mental health services furnished through telehealth
I
children.text: within the 6-month period prior to the first time such physician or practitioner furnishes such a telehealth service to the eligible telehealth individual; and

children.label: II
children.text: during subsequent periods in which such physician or practitioner furnishes such telehealth services to the eligible telehealth individual, at such times as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: under this paragraph (with respect to telehealth services furnished to an eligible telehealth individual with a substance use disorder diagnosis for purposes of treatment of such disorder or co-occurring mental health disorder); or

children.label: II
children.text: under this subsection without application of this paragraph.

children.heading: Enhancing telehealth services for Federally qualified health centers and rural health clinics
children.label: 8 Enhancing telehealth services for Federally qualified health centers and rural health clinics
i
children.text: the Secretary shall pay for telehealth services that are furnished via a telecommunications system by a Federally qualified health center or a rural health clinic to an eligible telehealth individual enrolled under this part notwithstanding that the Federally qualified health center or rural clinic providing the telehealth service is not at the same location as the beneficiary;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of payment to a Federally qualified health center or rural health clinic that serves as a distant site for such a telehealth service shall be determined under subparagraph (B); and

children.label: I
children.text: the term “distant site” includes a Federally qualified health center or rural health clinic that furnishes a telehealth service to an eligible telehealth individual; and

children.label: II
children.text: the term “telehealth services” includes a rural health clinic service or Federally qualified health center service that is furnished using telehealth to the extent that payment codes corresponding to services identified by the Secretary under clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (4)(F) are listed on the corresponding claim for such rural health clinic service or Federally qualified health center service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and implement payment methods that apply under this subsection to a Federally qualified health center or rural health clinic that serves as a distant site that furnishes a telehealth service to an eligible telehealth individual during the periods for which subparagraph (A) applies. Such payment methods shall be based on payment rates that are similar to the national average payment rates for comparable telehealth services under the physician fee schedule under . Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement such payment methods through program instruction or otherwise.section 1395w–4 of this title

children.heading: Exclusion from FQHC PPS calculation and RHC air calculation
children.label: ii Exclusion from FQHC PPS calculation and RHC air calculation
children.text: Costs associated with telehealth services shall not be used to determine the amount of payment for Federally qualified health center services under the prospective payment system under subsection () or for rural health clinic services under the methodology for all-inclusive rates (established by the Secretary) under section 1395(a)(3) of this title.ol

children.heading: Treatment of telehealth services furnished using audio-only telecommunications technology
children.label: 9 Treatment of telehealth services furnished using audio-only telecommunications technology
children.text: In the case that the emergency period described in ends before , the Secretary shall continue to provide coverage and payment under this part for telehealth services identified in paragraph (4)(F)(i) as of , that are furnished via an audio-only communications system during the period beginning on the first day after the end of such emergency period and ending on . For purposes of the previous sentence, the term “telehealth service” means a telehealth service identified as of , by a HCPCS code (and any succeeding codes) for which the Secretary has not applied the requirements of paragraph (1) and the first sentence of section 410.78(a)(3) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations, during such emergency period.section 1320b–5(g)(1)(B) of this titleDecember 31, 2024March 15, 2022December 31, 2027March 15, 2022
