children.label: 10 Required use of modifiers in certain instances
i
children.text: by a physician or practitioner that contracts with an entity that owns such virtual platform; or

children.label: ii
children.text: for which a physician or practitioner has a payment arrangement with an entity for use of such virtual platform; and

children.label: B
children.text: claims for telehealth services under this subsection that are furnished incident to a physician’s or practitioner’s professional service.

children.heading: Authority to modify or eliminate coverage of certain preventive services
children.label: n Authority to modify or eliminate coverage of certain preventive services
A
children.text: the coverage of any preventive service described in subparagraph (A) of to the extent that such modification is consistent with the recommendations of the United States Preventive Services Task Force; andsection 1395x(ddd)(3) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the services included in the initial preventive physical examination described in subparagraph (B) of such section; and
provide that no payment shall be made under this subchapter for a preventive service described in subparagraph (A) of such section that has not received a grade of A, B, C, or I by such Task Force.

children.heading: Development and implementation of prospective payment system
children.label: o Development and implementation of prospective payment system

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a prospective payment system for payment for Federally qualified health center services furnished by Federally qualified health centers under this subchapter. Such system shall include a process for appropriately describing the services furnished by Federally qualified health centers and shall establish payment rates for specific payment codes based on such appropriate descriptions of services. Such system shall be established to take into account the type, intensity, and duration of services furnished by Federally qualified health centers. Such system may include adjustments, including geographic adjustments, determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Collection of data and evaluation
children.label: B Collection of data and evaluation
children.text: By not later than , the Secretary shall require Federally qualified health centers to submit to the Secretary such information as the Secretary may require in order to develop and implement the prospective payment system under this subsection, including the reporting of services using HCPCS codes.January 1, 2011

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding section 1395(a)(3)(A) of this title, the Secretary shall provide, for cost reporting periods beginning on or after , for payments of prospective payment rates for Federally qualified health center services furnished by Federally qualified health centers under this subchapter in accordance with the prospective payment system developed by the Secretary under paragraph (1).lOctober 1, 2014

children.heading: Initial payments
children.label: i Initial payments
children.text: The Secretary shall implement such prospective payment system so that the estimated aggregate amount of prospective payment rates (determined prior to the application of section 1395(a)(1)(Z) of this title) under this subchapter for Federally qualified health center services in the first year that such system is implemented is equal to 100 percent of the estimated amount of reasonable costs (determined without the application of a per visit payment limit or productivity screen and prior to the application of ) that would have occurred for such services under this subchapter in such year if the system had not been implemented.lsection 1395cc(a)(2)(A)(ii) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: in the first year after implementation of such system, by the percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in ) for the year involved; andsection 1395u(i)(3) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: in subsequent years, by the percentage increase in a market basket of Federally qualified health center goods and services as promulgated through regulations, or if such an index is not available, by the percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in ) for the year involved.section 1395u(i)(3) of this title

children.heading: Preparation for PPS implementation
children.label: C Preparation for PPS implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may establish and implement by program instruction or otherwise the payment codes to be used under the prospective payment system under this section.

children.heading: Additional payments for certain FQHCS with physicians or other practitioners receiving data 2000 waivers
children.label: 3 Additional payments for certain FQHCS with physicians or other practitioners receiving data 2000 waivers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a Federally qualified health center with respect to which, beginning on or after , Federally qualified health center services (as defined in ) are furnished for the treatment of opioid use disorder by a physician or practitioner who meets the requirements described in subparagraph (C), the Secretary shall, subject to availability of funds under subparagraph (D), make a payment (at such time and in such manner as specified by the Secretary) to such Federally qualified health center after receiving and approving an application submitted by such Federally qualified health center under subparagraph (B). Such a payment shall be in an amount determined by the Secretary, based on an estimate of the average costs of training for purposes of receiving a waiver described in subparagraph (C)(ii). Such a payment may be made only one time with respect to each such physician or practitioner.January 1, 2019section 1395x(aa)(3) of this title

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: In order to receive a payment described in subparagraph (A), a Federally qualified health center shall submit to the Secretary an application for such a payment at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as specified by the Secretary. A Federally qualified health center may apply for such a payment for each physician or practitioner described in subparagraph (A) furnishing services described in such subparagraph at such center.

children.label: i
children.text: The physician or practitioner is employed by or working under contract with a Federally qualified health center described in subparagraph (A) that submits an application under subparagraph (B).

children.label: ii
children.text: The physician or practitioner first begins prescribing narcotic drugs in schedule III, IV, or V of for the purpose of maintenance or detoxification treatment on or after .section 812 of title 21January 1, 2021

children.heading: Funding
children.label: D Funding
children.text: For purposes of making payments under this paragraph, there are appropriated, out of amounts in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $6,000,000, which shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: Payment for certain services furnished by Federally qualified health centers
children.label: 4 Payment for certain services furnished by Federally qualified health centers

children.heading: Attending physician services for hospice patients
children.label: A Attending physician services for hospice patients
children.text: In the case of services described in furnished on or after , by an attending physician (as defined in , other than a physician or practitioner who is employed by a hospice program) who is employed by or working under contract with a Federally qualified health center, a Federally qualified health center shall be paid for such services under the prospective payment system under this subsection.section 1395d(d)(2)(A)(ii) of this titlesection 1395x(dd)(3)(B) of this titleJanuary 1, 2022

children.heading: Mental health visits furnished via telecommunications technology
children.label: B Mental health visits furnished via telecommunications technology
children.text: In the case of mental health visits furnished via interactive, real-time, audio and video telecommunications technology or audio-only interactions, the in-person mental health visit requirements established under section 405.2463(b)(3) of title 42 of the Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation) shall not apply prior to .January 1, 2028

children.heading: Special payment rule for intensive outpatient services
children.label: 5 Special payment rule for intensive outpatient services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of intensive outpatient services furnished by a Federally qualified health center, the payment amount for such services shall be equal to the amount that would have been paid under this subchapter for such services had such services been covered OPD services furnished by a hospital.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: Costs associated with intensive outpatient services shall not be used to determine the amount of payment for Federally qualified health center services under the prospective payment system under this subsection.

children.heading: Quality incentives to promote patient safety and public health in computed tomography
children.label: p Quality incentives to promote patient safety and public health in computed tomography

children.heading: Quality incentives
children.label: 1 Quality incentives
children.text: In the case of an applicable computed tomography service (as defined in paragraph (2)) for which payment is made under an applicable payment system (as defined in paragraph (3)) and that is furnished on or after , using equipment that is not consistent with the CT equipment standard (described in paragraph (4)), the payment amount for such service shall be reduced by the applicable percentage (as defined in paragraph (5)).January 1, 2016

children.heading: Applicable computed tomography services defined
children.label: 2 Applicable computed tomography services defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “applicable computed tomography service” means a service billed using diagnostic radiological imaging codes for computed tomography (identified as of , by HCPCS codes 70450–70498, 71250–71275, 72125–72133, 72191–72194, 73200–73206, 73700–73706, 74150–74178, 74261–74263, and 75571–75574 (and any succeeding codes).January 1, 20146So in original. The period probably should be preceded by another closing parenthesis.6

children.label: A
children.text: The technical component and the technical component of the global fee under the fee schedule established under .section 1395w–4(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The prospective payment system for hospital outpatient department services under section 1395(t) of this title.l

children.heading: Consistency with CT equipment standard
children.label: 4 Consistency with CT equipment standard
children.text: In this subsection, the term “not consistent with the CT equipment standard” means, with respect to an applicable computed tomography service, that the service was furnished using equipment that does not meet each of the attributes of the National Electrical Manufacturers Association (NEMA) Standard XR–29–2013, entitled “Standard Attributes on CT Equipment Related to Dose Optimization and Management”. Through rulemaking, the Secretary may apply successor standards.

children.label: A
children.text: for 2016, 5 percent; and

children.label: B
children.text: for 2017 and subsequent years, 15 percent.

children.heading: Information
children.label: A Information
children.text: The Secretary shall require that information be provided and attested to by a supplier and a hospital outpatient department that indicates whether an applicable computed tomography service was furnished that was not consistent with the CT equipment standard (described in paragraph (4)). Such information may be included on a claim and may be a modifier. Such information shall be verified, as appropriate, as part of the periodic accreditation of suppliers under subsection (e) and hospitals under .section 1395bb(a) of this title

children.heading: Administration
children.label: B Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to information described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Recognizing appropriate use criteria for certain imaging services
children.label: q Recognizing appropriate use criteria for certain imaging services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to promote the use of appropriate use criteria (as defined in subparagraph (B)) for applicable imaging services (as defined in subparagraph (C)) furnished in an applicable setting (as defined in subparagraph (D)) by ordering professionals and furnishing professionals (as defined in subparagraphs (E) and (F), respectively).

children.heading: Appropriate use criteria defined
children.label: B Appropriate use criteria defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “appropriate use criteria” means criteria, only developed or endorsed by national professional medical specialty societies or other provider-led entities, to assist ordering professionals and furnishing professionals in making the most appropriate treatment decision for a specific clinical condition for an individual. To the extent feasible, such criteria shall be evidence-based.

children.label: i
children.text: one or more applicable appropriate use criteria specified under paragraph (2) apply;

children.label: ii
children.text: there are one or more qualified clinical decision support mechanisms listed under paragraph (3)(C); and

children.label: iii
children.text: one or more of such mechanisms is available free of charge.

children.heading: Applicable setting defined
children.label: D Applicable setting defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “applicable setting” means a physician’s office, a hospital outpatient department (including an emergency department), an ambulatory surgical center, and any other provider-led outpatient setting determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Ordering professional defined
children.label: E Ordering professional defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “ordering professional” means a physician (as defined in ) or a practitioner described in who orders an applicable imaging service.section 1395x(r) of this titlesection 1395u(b)(18)(C) of this title

children.heading: Furnishing professional defined
children.label: F Furnishing professional defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “furnishing professional” means a physician (as defined in ) or a practitioner described in who furnishes an applicable imaging service.section 1395x(r) of this titlesection 1395u(b)(18)(C) of this title

children.heading: Establishment of applicable appropriate use criteria
children.label: 2 Establishment of applicable appropriate use criteria

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall through rulemaking, and in consultation with physicians, practitioners, and other stakeholders, specify applicable appropriate use criteria for applicable imaging services only from among appropriate use criteria developed or endorsed by national professional medical specialty societies or other provider-led entities.November 15, 2015

children.label: i
children.text: have stakeholder consensus;

children.label: ii
children.text: are scientifically valid and evidence based; and

children.label: iii
children.text: are based on studies that are published and reviewable by stakeholders.

children.heading: Revisions
children.label: C Revisions
children.text: The Secretary shall review, on an annual basis, the specified applicable appropriate use criteria to determine if there is a need to update or revise (as appropriate) such specification of applicable appropriate use criteria and make such updates or revisions through rulemaking.

children.heading: Treatment of multiple applicable appropriate use criteria
children.label: D Treatment of multiple applicable appropriate use criteria
children.text: In the case where the Secretary determines that more than one appropriate use criterion applies with respect to an applicable imaging service, the Secretary shall apply one or more applicable appropriate use criteria under this paragraph for the service.

children.heading: Mechanisms for consultation with applicable appropriate use criteria
children.label: 3 Mechanisms for consultation with applicable appropriate use criteria

children.heading: Identification of mechanisms to consult with applicable appropriate use criteria
children.label: A Identification of mechanisms to consult with applicable appropriate use criteria

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall specify qualified clinical decision support mechanisms that could be used by ordering professionals to consult with applicable appropriate use criteria for applicable imaging services.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: ii Consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with physicians, practitioners, health care technology experts, and other stakeholders in specifying mechanisms under this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: Use of clinical decision support modules in certified EHR technology (as defined in section 1395w–4()(4) of this title).o

children.label: II
children.text: Use of private sector clinical decision support mechanisms that are independent from certified EHR technology, which may include use of clinical decision support mechanisms available from medical specialty organizations.

children.label: III
children.text: Use of a clinical decision support mechanism established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Qualified clinical decision support mechanisms
children.label: B Qualified clinical decision support mechanisms

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a qualified clinical decision support mechanism is a mechanism that the Secretary determines meets the requirements described in clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: The mechanism makes available to the ordering professional applicable appropriate use criteria specified under paragraph (2) and the supporting documentation for the applicable imaging service ordered.

children.label: II
children.text: In the case where there is more than one applicable appropriate use criterion specified under such paragraph for an applicable imaging service, the mechanism indicates the criteria that it uses for the service.

children.label: III
children.text: The mechanism determines the extent to which an applicable imaging service ordered is consistent with the applicable appropriate use criteria so specified.

children.label: IV
children.text: The mechanism generates and provides to the ordering professional a certification or documentation that documents that the qualified clinical decision support mechanism was consulted by the ordering professional.

children.label: V
children.text: The mechanism is updated on a timely basis to reflect revisions to the specification of applicable appropriate use criteria under such paragraph.

children.label: VI
children.text: The mechanism meets privacy and security standards under applicable provisions of law.

children.label: VII
children.text: The mechanism performs such other functions as specified by the Secretary, which may include a requirement to provide aggregate feedback to the ordering professional.

children.heading: List of mechanisms for consultation with applicable appropriate use criteria
children.label: C List of mechanisms for consultation with applicable appropriate use criteria

children.heading: Initial list
children.label: i Initial list
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall publish a list of mechanisms specified under this paragraph.April 1, 2016

children.heading: Periodic updating of list
children.label: ii Periodic updating of list
children.text: The Secretary shall identify on an annual basis the list of qualified clinical decision support mechanisms specified under this paragraph.

children.heading: Consultation with applicable appropriate use criteria
children.label: 4 Consultation with applicable appropriate use criteria
i
children.text: consult with a qualified decision support mechanism listed under paragraph (3)(C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide to the furnishing professional the information described in clauses (i) through (iii) of subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: Information about which qualified clinical decision support mechanism was consulted by the ordering professional for the service.

children.label: I
children.text: whether the service ordered would adhere to the applicable appropriate use criteria specified under paragraph (2);

children.label: II
children.text: whether the service ordered would not adhere to such criteria; or

children.label: III
children.text: whether such criteria was not applicable to the service ordered.

children.label: iii
children.text: The national provider identifier of the ordering professional (if different from the furnishing professional).

children.heading: Emergency services
children.label: i Emergency services
children.text: An applicable imaging service ordered for an individual with an emergency medical condition (as defined in ).section 1395dd(e)(1) of this title

children.heading: Inpatient services
children.label: ii Inpatient services
children.text: An applicable imaging service ordered for an inpatient and for which payment is made under part A.

children.heading: Significant hardship
children.label: iii Significant hardship
children.text: An applicable imaging service ordered by an ordering professional who the Secretary may, on a case-by-case basis, exempt from the application of such provisions if the Secretary determines, subject to annual renewal, that consultation with applicable appropriate use criteria would result in a significant hardship, such as in the case of a professional who practices in a rural area without sufficient Internet access.

children.label: i
children.text: The physician fee schedule established under .section 1395w–4(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The prospective payment system for hospital outpatient department services under section 1395(t) of this title.l

children.label: iii
children.text: The ambulatory surgical center payment systems under section 1395(i) of this title.l

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to applicable imaging services furnished beginning with 2017, the Secretary shall determine, on an annual basis, no more than five percent of the total number of ordering professionals who are outlier ordering professionals.

children.label: i
children.text: be based on low adherence to applicable appropriate use criteria specified under paragraph (2), which may be based on comparison to other ordering professionals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include data for ordering professionals for whom prior authorization under paragraph (6)(A) applies.

children.heading: Use of two years of data
children.label: C Use of two years of data
children.text: The Secretary shall use two years of data to identify outlier ordering professionals under this paragraph.

children.heading: Process
children.label: D Process
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a process for determining when an outlier ordering professional is no longer an outlier ordering professional.

children.heading: Consultation with stakeholders
children.label: E Consultation with stakeholders
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with physicians, practitioners and other stakeholders in developing methods to identify outlier ordering professionals under this paragraph.

children.heading: Prior authorization for ordering professionals who are outliers
children.label: 6 Prior authorization for ordering professionals who are outliers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning , subject to paragraph (4)(C), with respect to services furnished during a year, the Secretary shall, for a period determined appropriate by the Secretary, apply prior authorization for applicable imaging services that are ordered by an outlier ordering professional identified under paragraph (5).January 1, 2020

children.heading: Appropriate use criteria in prior authorization
children.label: B Appropriate use criteria in prior authorization
children.text: In applying prior authorization under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall utilize only the applicable appropriate use criteria specified under this subsection.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: C Funding
children.text: For purposes of carrying out this paragraph, the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under , of $5,000,000 to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2021. Amounts transferred under the preceding sentence shall remain available until expended.section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 7 Construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as granting the Secretary the authority to develop or initiate the development of clinical practice guidelines or appropriate use criteria.

children.heading: Payment for renal dialysis services for individuals with acute kidney injury
children.label: r Payment for renal dialysis services for individuals with acute kidney injury

children.heading: Payment rate
children.label: 1 Payment rate
children.text: In the case of renal dialysis services (as defined in subparagraph (B) of ) furnished under this part by a renal dialysis facility or provider of services paid under such section during a year (beginning with 2017) to an individual with acute kidney injury (as defined in paragraph (2)), the amount of payment under this part for such services shall be the base rate for renal dialysis services determined for such year under such section, as adjusted by any applicable geographic adjustment factor applied under subparagraph (D)(iv)(II) of such section and may be adjusted by the Secretary (on a budget neutral basis for payments under this paragraph) by any other adjustment factor under subparagraph (D) of such section.section 1395rr(b)(14) of this title

children.heading: Individual with acute kidney injury defined
children.label: 2 Individual with acute kidney injury defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “individual with acute kidney injury” means an individual who has acute loss of renal function and does not receive renal dialysis services for which payment is made under .section 1395rr(b)(14) of this title

children.heading: Payment for applicable disposable devices
children.label: s Payment for applicable disposable devices

children.heading: Separate payment
children.label: 1 Separate payment
children.text: The Secretary shall make a payment (separate from the payments otherwise made under ) in the amount established under paragraph (3) to a home health agency for an applicable disposable device (as defined in paragraph (2)) when furnished on or after , to an individual who receives home health services for which payment is made under .section 1395fff of this titlesection 1395fff(b) of this titleJanuary 1, 2017

children.label: A
children.text: a disposable negative pressure wound therapy device that is an integrated system comprised of a non-manual vacuum pump, a receptacle for collecting exudate, and dressings for the purposes of wound therapy; and

children.label: B
children.text: a substitute for, and used in lieu of, a negative pressure wound therapy durable medical equipment item that is an integrated system of a negative pressure vacuum pump, a separate exudate collection canister, and dressings that would otherwise be covered for individuals for such wound therapy.

children.label: i
children.text: for a year before 2024, the amount of the payment that would be made under section 1395(t) of this title (relating to payment for covered OPD services) for the year for the Level I Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) code for which the description for a professional service includes the furnishing of such device;l

children.label: ii
children.text: for 2024, the supply price used to determine the relative value for the service under the fee schedule under (as of ) for the applicable disposable device, updated by the specified adjustment described in subparagraph (B) for such year; andsection 1395w–4 of this titleJanuary 1, 2022

children.label: iii
children.text: for 2025 and each subsequent year, the payment amount established under this paragraph for such device for the previous year, updated by the specified adjustment described in subparagraph (B) for such year.

children.label: I
children.text: the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending in June of the previous year; minus

children.label: II
children.text: the productivity adjustment described in for such year.section 1395ww(b)(3)(B)(xi)(II) of this title

children.heading: Clarification on application of the productivity adjustment
children.label: ii Clarification on application of the productivity adjustment
children.text: The application of clause (i)(II) may result in a specified adjustment of less than 0.0 for a year, and may result in the separate payment amount under this subsection for an applicable device for a year being less than such separate payment amount for such device for the preceding year.

children.heading: Exclusion of nursing and therapy services from separate payment
children.label: C Exclusion of nursing and therapy services from separate payment
children.text: With respect to applicable devices furnished on or after , the separate payment amount determined under this paragraph shall not include payment for nursing or therapy services described in . Payment for such nursing or therapy services shall be made under the prospective payment system established under and shall not be separately billable.January 1, 2024section 1395x(m) of this titlesection 1395fff of this title

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 4 Implementation
children.text: As part of submitting claims for the separate payment established under this subsection, beginning with 2024, the Secretary shall accept and process claims submitted using the type of bill that is most commonly used by home health agencies to bill services under a home health plan of care.

children.label: A
children.text: the estimated payment amount for the item or service under the outpatient department fee schedule under subsection (t) of section 1395 of this title and the ambulatory surgical center payment system under subsection (i) of such section; andl

children.label: B
children.text: the estimated amount of beneficiary liability applicable to the item or service.

children.heading: Calculation of estimated beneficiary liability
children.label: 2 Calculation of estimated beneficiary liability
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(B), the estimated amount of beneficiary liability, with respect to an item or service, is the amount for such item or service for which an individual who does not have coverage under a Medicare supplemental policy certified under or any other supplemental insurance coverage is responsible.section 1395ss of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall include in the notice described in a notification of the availability of the estimated amounts made available under paragraph (1); andsection 1395b–2(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: may utilize mechanisms in existence on , such as the portion of the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services on which information comparing physician performance is posted (commonly referred to as the Physician Compare Internet website), to make available such estimated amounts under such paragraph.December 13, 2016

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 4 Funding
children.text: For purposes of implementing this subsection, the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account, of $6,000,000 for fiscal year 2017, to remain available until expended.section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Payment and related requirements for home infusion therapy
children.label: u Payment and related requirements for home infusion therapy

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (iii) and subparagraphs (B) and (C), the Secretary shall implement a payment system under which a single payment is made under this subchapter to a qualified home infusion therapy supplier for items and services described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 1395x(iii)(2)) of this title furnished by a qualified home infusion therapy supplier (as defined in ) in coordination with the furnishing of home infusion drugs (as defined in ) under this part.7section 1395x(iii)(3)(D) of this titlesection 1395x(iii)(3)(C) of this titleSo in original. The second closing parenthesis probably should not appear.7

children.heading: Unit of single payment
children.label: ii Unit of single payment
children.text: A unit of single payment under the payment system implemented under this subparagraph is for each infusion drug administration calendar day in the individual’s home. The Secretary shall, as appropriate, establish single payment amounts for types of infusion therapy, including to take into account variation in utilization of nursing services by therapy type.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iii Limitation
children.text: The single payment amount determined under this subparagraph after application of subparagraph (B) and paragraph (3) shall not exceed the amount determined under the fee schedule under for infusion therapy services furnished in a calendar day if furnished in a physician office setting, except such single payment shall not reflect more than 5 hours of infusion for a particular therapy in a calendar day.section 1395w–4 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a geographic wage index and other costs that may vary by region; and

children.label: ii
children.text: patient acuity and complexity of drug administration.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the Secretary may adjust the single payment amount determined under subparagraph (A) (after application of subparagraph (B)) to reflect outlier situations and other factors as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Requirement of budget neutrality
children.label: ii Requirement of budget neutrality
children.text: Any adjustment under this subparagraph shall be made in a budget neutral manner.

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: 2 Considerations
children.text: In developing the payment system under this subsection, the Secretary may consider the costs of furnishing infusion therapy in the home, consult with home infusion therapy suppliers, consider payment amounts for similar items and services under this part and part A, and consider payment amounts established by Medicare Advantage plans under part C and in the private insurance market for home infusion therapy (including average per treatment day payment amounts by type of home infusion therapy).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall update the single payment amount under this subsection from year to year beginning in 2022 by increasing the single payment amount from the prior year by the percentage increase in the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the preceding year.

children.heading: Adjustment
children.label: B Adjustment
children.text: For each year, the Secretary shall reduce the percentage increase described in subparagraph (A) by the productivity adjustment described in . The application of the preceding sentence may result in a percentage being less than 0.0 for a year, and may result in payment being less than such payment rates for the preceding year.section 1395ww(b)(3)(B)(xi)(II) of this title

children.heading: Authority to apply prior authorization
children.label: 4 Authority to apply prior authorization
children.text: The Secretary may, as determined appropriate by the Secretary, apply prior authorization for home infusion therapy services under .section 1395x(iii)(1) of this title

children.heading: Accreditation of qualified home infusion therapy suppliers
children.label: 5 Accreditation of qualified home infusion therapy suppliers

children.heading: Factors for designation of accreditation organizations
children.label: A Factors for designation of accreditation organizations
i
children.text: The ability of the organization to conduct timely reviews of accreditation applications.

children.label: ii
children.text: The ability of the organization to take into account the capacities of suppliers located in a rural area (as defined in ).section 1395ww(d)(2)(D) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether the organization has established reasonable fees to be charged to suppliers applying for accreditation.

children.label: iv
children.text: Such other factors as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: B Designation
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall designate organizations to accredit suppliers furnishing home infusion therapy. The list of accreditation organizations so designated may be modified pursuant to subparagraph (C).January 1, 2021

children.heading: Review and modification of list of accreditation organizations
children.label: C Review and modification of list of accreditation organizations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall review the list of accreditation organizations designated under subparagraph (B) taking into account the factors under subparagraph (A). Taking into account the results of such review, the Secretary may, by regulation, modify the list of accreditation organizations designated under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Special rule for accreditations done prior to removal from list of designated accreditation organizations
children.label: ii Special rule for accreditations done prior to removal from list of designated accreditation organizations
children.text: In the case where the Secretary removes an organization from the list of accreditation organizations designated under subparagraph (B), any supplier that is accredited by the organization during the period beginning on the date on which the organization is designated as an accreditation organization under subparagraph (B) and ending on the date on which the organization is removed from such list shall be considered to have been accredited by an organization designated by the Secretary under subparagraph (B) for the remaining period such accreditation is in effect.

children.heading: Rule for accreditations made prior to designation
children.label: D Rule for accreditations made prior to designation
children.text: In the case of a supplier that is accredited before , by an accreditation organization designated by the Secretary under subparagraph (B) as of , such supplier shall be considered to have been accredited by an organization designated by the Secretary under such paragraph as of , for the remaining period such accreditation is in effect.January 1, 2021January 1, 2019January 1, 2023

children.heading: Notification of infusion therapy options available prior to furnishing home infusion therapy
children.label: 6 Notification of infusion therapy options available prior to furnishing home infusion therapy
children.text: Prior to the furnishing of home infusion therapy to an individual, the physician who establishes the plan described in for the individual shall provide notification (in a form, manner, and frequency determined appropriate by the Secretary) of the options available (such as home, physician’s office, hospital outpatient department) for the furnishing of infusion therapy under this part.section 1395x(iii)(1) of this title

children.heading: Home infusion therapy services temporary transitional payment
children.label: 7 Home infusion therapy services temporary transitional payment

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, in accordance with the payment methodology described in subparagraph (B) and subject to the provisions of this paragraph, provide a home infusion therapy services temporary transitional payment under this part to an eligible home infusion supplier (as defined in subparagraph (F)) for items and services described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 1395x(iii)(2)) of this title furnished during the period specified in clause (ii) by such supplier in coordination with the furnishing of transitional home infusion drugs (as defined in clause (iii)).7

children.heading: Period specified
children.label: ii Period specified
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the period specified in this clause is the period beginning on , and ending on the day before the date of the implementation of the payment system under paragraph (1)(A).January 1, 2019

children.heading: Transitional home infusion drug defined
children.label: iii Transitional home infusion drug defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “transitional home infusion drug” has the meaning given to the term “home infusion drug” under section 1395x(iii)(3)(C)) of this title, except that clause (ii) of such section shall not apply if a drug described in such clause is identified in clauses (i), (ii), (iii) or (iv) of subparagraph (C) as of .7February 9, 2018

children.label: i
children.text: create the three payment categories described in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of subparagraph (C);

children.label: ii
children.text: assign drugs to such categories, in accordance with such clauses;

children.label: iii
children.text: assign appropriate Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) codes to each payment category; and

children.label: iv
children.text: establish a single payment amount for each such payment category, in accordance with subparagraph (D), for each infusion drug administration calendar day in the individual’s home for drugs assigned to such category.

children.heading: Payment category 1
children.label: i Payment category 1
children.text: The Secretary shall create a payment category 1 and assign to such category drugs which are covered under the Local Coverage Determination on External Infusion Pumps (LCD number L33794) and billed with the following HCPCS codes (as identified as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary): J0133, J0285, J0287, J0288, J0289, J0895, J1170, J1250, J1265, J1325, J1455, J1457, J1570, J2175, J2260, J2270, J2274, J2278, J3010, or J3285.January 1, 2018

children.heading: Payment category 2
children.label: ii Payment category 2
children.text: The Secretary shall create a payment category 2 and assign to such category drugs which are covered under such local coverage determination and billed with the following HCPCS codes (as identified as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary): J1555 JB, J1559 JB, J1561 JB, J1562 JB, J1569 JB, or J1575 JB.January 1, 2018

children.heading: Payment category 3
children.label: iii Payment category 3
children.text: The Secretary shall create a payment category 3 and assign to such category drugs which are covered under such local coverage determination and billed with the following HCPCS codes (as identified as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary): J9000, J9039, J9040, J9065, J9100, J9190, J9200, J9360, or J9370.January 1, 2018

children.heading: Infusion drugs not otherwise included
children.label: iv Infusion drugs not otherwise included
I
children.text: covered under such local coverage determination and billed under HCPCS codes J7799 or J7999 (as identified as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary); orJuly 1, 2017

children.label: II
children.text: billed under any code that is implemented after , and included in such local coverage determination or included in subregulatory guidance as a home infusion drug described in subparagraph (A)(i).February 9, 2018

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Under the payment methodology, the Secretary shall pay eligible home infusion suppliers, with respect to items and services described in subparagraph (A)(i) furnished during the period described in subparagraph (A)(ii) by such supplier to an individual, at amounts equal to the amounts determined under the physician fee schedule established under for services furnished during the year for codes and units of such codes described in clauses (ii), (iii), and (iv) with respect to drugs included in the payment category under subparagraph (C) specified in the respective clause, determined without application of the geographic adjustment under subsection (e) of such section.section 1395w–4 of this title

children.heading: Payment amount for category 1
children.label: ii Payment amount for category 1
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the codes and units described in this clause, with respect to drugs included in payment category 1 described in subparagraph (C)(i), are one unit of HCPCS code 96365 plus three units of HCPCS code 96366 (as identified as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary).January 1, 2018

children.heading: Payment amount for category 2
children.label: iii Payment amount for category 2
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the codes and units described in this clause, with respect to drugs included in payment category 2 described in subparagraph (C)(i), are one unit of HCPCS code 96369 plus three units of HCPCS code 96370 (as identified as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary).January 1, 2018

children.heading: Payment amount for category 3
children.label: iv Payment amount for category 3
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the codes and units described in this clause, with respect to drugs included in payment category 3 described in subparagraph (C)(i), are one unit of HCPCS code 96413 plus three units of HCPCS code 96415 (as identified as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary).January 1, 2018

children.heading: Infusion drug administration day
children.label: i Infusion drug administration day
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, with respect to the furnishing of transitional home infusion drugs or home infusion drugs to an individual by an eligible home infusion supplier or a qualified home infusion therapy supplier, a reference to payment to such supplier for an infusion drug administration calendar day in the individual’s home shall refer to payment only for the date on which professional services (as described in ) were furnished to administer such drugs to such individual. For purposes of the previous sentence, an infusion drug administration calendar day shall include all such drugs administered to such individual on such day.section 1395x(iii)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of multiple drugs administered on same infusion drug administration day
children.label: ii Treatment of multiple drugs administered on same infusion drug administration day
children.text: In the case that an eligible home infusion supplier, with respect to an infusion drug administration calendar day in an individual’s home, furnishes to such individual transitional home infusion drugs which are not all assigned to the same payment category under subparagraph (C), payment to such supplier for such infusion drug administration calendar day in the individual’s home shall be a single payment equal to the amount of payment under this paragraph for the drug, among all such drugs so furnished to such individual during such calendar day, for which the highest payment would be made under this paragraph.

children.heading: Eligible home infusion suppliers
children.label: F Eligible home infusion suppliers
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “eligible home infusion supplier” means a supplier that is enrolled under this part as a pharmacy that provides external infusion pumps and external infusion pump supplies and that maintains all pharmacy licensure requirements in the State in which the applicable infusion drugs are administered.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: G Implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement this paragraph by program instruction or otherwise.

children.heading: Payment for outpatient physical therapy services and outpatient occupational therapy services furnished by a therapy assistant
children.label: v Payment for outpatient physical therapy services and outpatient occupational therapy services furnished by a therapy assistant

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of an outpatient physical therapy service or outpatient occupational therapy service furnished on or after , for which payment is made under or subsection (k), that is furnished in whole or in part by a therapy assistant (as defined by the Secretary), the amount of payment for such service shall be an amount equal to 85 percent of the amount of payment otherwise applicable for the service under this part. Nothing in the preceding sentence shall be construed to change applicable requirements with respect to such services.January 1, 2022section 1395w–4 of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish a modifier to indicate (in a form and manner specified by the Secretary), in the case of an outpatient physical therapy service or outpatient occupational therapy service furnished in whole or in part by a therapy assistant (as so defined), that the service was furnished by a therapy assistant.January 1, 2019

children.heading: Required use
children.label: B Required use
children.text: Each request for payment, or bill submitted, for an outpatient physical therapy service or outpatient occupational therapy service furnished in whole or in part by a therapy assistant (as so defined) on or after , shall include the modifier established under subparagraph (A) for each such service.January 1, 2020

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 3 Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall implement this subsection through notice and comment rulemaking.

children.heading: Opioid use disorder treatment services
children.label: w Opioid use disorder treatment services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to an opioid treatment program (as defined in paragraph (2) of ) an amount that is equal to 100 percent of a bundled payment under this part for opioid use disorder treatment services (as defined in paragraph (1) of such section) that are furnished by such program to an individual during an episode of care (as defined by the Secretary) beginning on or after . The Secretary shall ensure, as determined appropriate by the Secretary, that no duplicative payments are made under this part or part D for items and services furnished by an opioid treatment program.section 1395x(jjj) of this titleJanuary 1, 2020

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: 2 Considerations
children.text: The Secretary may implement this subsection through one or more bundles based on the type of medication provided (such as buprenorphine, methadone, naltrexone, or a new innovative drug), the frequency of services, the scope of services furnished, characteristics of the individuals furnished such services, or other factors as the Secretary determine appropriate. In developing such bundles, the Secretary may consider payment rates paid to opioid treatment programs for comparable services under State plans under subchapter XIX or under the TRICARE program under chapter 55 of title 10.8So in original. Probably should be “determines”.8

children.heading: Annual updates
children.label: 3 Annual updates
children.text: The Secretary shall provide an update each year to the bundled payment amounts under this subsection.

children.heading: Payment rules relating to rural emergency hospitals
children.label: x Payment rules relating to rural emergency hospitals

children.heading: Payment for rural emergency hospital services
children.label: 1 Payment for rural emergency hospital services
children.text: In the case of rural emergency hospital services (as defined in ), furnished by a rural emergency hospital (as defined in ) on or after , the amount of payment for such services shall be equal to the amount of payment that would otherwise apply under section 1395(t) of this title for covered OPD services (as defined in section 1395(t)(1)(B) of this title (other than clause (ii) of such section)), increased by 5 percent to reflect the higher costs incurred by such hospitals, and shall include the application of any copayment amount determined under section 1395(t)(8) of this title as if such increase had not occurred.section 1395x(kkk)(1) of this titlesection 1395x(kkk)(2) of this titleJanuary 1, 2023lll

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make monthly payments to a rural emergency hospital in an amount that is equal to ⁄ of the annual additional facility payment specified in subparagraph (B).112

children.heading: Annual additional facility payment amount
children.label: B Annual additional facility payment amount
i
children.text: for 2023, a Medicare subsidy amount determined under subparagraph (C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: for 2024 and each subsequent year, the amount determined under this subparagraph for the preceding year, increased by the hospital market basket percentage increase.

children.heading: Determination of medicare subsidy amount
children.label: C Determination of medicare subsidy amount
I
children.text: the total amount that the Secretary determines was paid under this subchapter to all critical access hospitals in 2019; over

children.label: II
children.text: the estimated total amount that the Secretary determines would have been paid under this subchapter to such hospitals in 2019 if payment were made for inpatient hospital, outpatient hospital, and skilled nursing facility services under the applicable prospective payment systems for such services during such year; divided by

children.label: ii
children.text: the total number of such hospitals in 2019.

children.heading: Reporting on use of the additional facility payment
children.label: D Reporting on use of the additional facility payment
children.text: A rural emergency hospital receiving the additional facility payment under this paragraph shall maintain detailed information as specified by the Secretary as to how the facility has used the additional facility payments. Such information shall be made available to the Secretary upon request.

children.heading: Payment for ambulance services
children.label: 3 Payment for ambulance services
children.text: For provisions relating to payment for ambulance services furnished by an entity owned and operated by a rural emergency hospital, see subsection ().l

children.heading: Payment for post-hospital extended care services
children.label: 4 Payment for post-hospital extended care services
children.text: For provisions relating to payment for post-hospital extended care services furnished by a rural emergency hospital that has a unit that is a distinct part licensed as a skilled nursing facility, see .section 1395yy(e) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), payments under this subsection shall be made from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under .section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Additional facility payment and post-hospital extended care services
children.label: B Additional facility payment and post-hospital extended care services
children.text: Payments under paragraph (2) shall be made from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund under .section 1395i of this title

children.heading: Payment for certain services furnished by rural health clinics
children.label: y Payment for certain services furnished by rural health clinics

children.heading: Attending physician services for hospice patients
children.label: 1 Attending physician services for hospice patients
children.text: In the case of services described in furnished on or after , by an attending physician (as defined in , other than a physician or practitioner who is employed by a hospice program) who is employed by or working under contract with a rural health clinic, a rural health clinic shall be paid for such services under the methodology for all-inclusive rates (established by the Secretary) under section 1395(a)(3) of this title, subject to the limits described in section 1395(f) of this title.section 1395d(d)(2)(A)(ii) of this titlesection 1395x(dd)(3)(B) of this titleJanuary 1, 2022ll

children.heading: Mental health visits furnished via telecommunications technology
children.label: 2 Mental health visits furnished via telecommunications technology
children.text: In the case of mental health visits furnished via interactive, real-time, audio and video telecommunications technology or audio-only interactions, the in-person mental health visit requirements established under section 405.2463(b)(3) of title 42 of the Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation) shall not apply prior to .January 1, 2028

children.heading: Special payment rule for intensive outpatient services
children.label: 3 Special payment rule for intensive outpatient services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of intensive outpatient services furnished by a rural health clinic, the payment amount for such services shall be equal to the amount that would have been paid under this subchapter for such services had such services been covered OPD services furnished by a hospital.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: Costs associated with intensive outpatient services shall not be used to determine the amount of payment for rural health clinic services under the methodology for all-inclusive rates (established by the Secretary) under section 1395(a)(3) of this title.l

children.heading: Payment for lymphedema compression treatment items
children.label: z Payment for lymphedema compression treatment items

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine an appropriate payment basis for lymphedema compression treatment items (as defined in ). In making such a determination, the Secretary may take into account payment rates for such items under State plans (or waivers of such plans) under subchapter XIX, the Veterans Health Administration, and group health plans and health insurance coverage (as such terms are defined in ), and such other information as the Secretary determines appropriate.section 1395x(mmm) of this titlesection 300gg–91 of this title

children.heading: Frequency limitation
children.label: 2 Frequency limitation
children.text: No payment may be made under this part for lymphedema compression treatment items furnished other than at such frequency as the Secretary may establish.

children.label: A
children.text: the payment basis under this subsection for such items furnished in such area shall be the payment basis determined under such competitive acquisition program; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary may use information on the payment determined under such competitive acquisition programs to adjust the payment amount otherwise determined under this subsection for an area that is not a competitive acquisition area under , and in the case of such adjustment, paragraphs (8) and (9) of shall not be applied.section 1395w–3 of this titlesection 1395u(b) of this title

children.heading: Standards following USPSTF rating of A or B
children.label: C Standards following USPSTF rating of A or B
children.text: In the case of a multi-cancer early detection screening test that is recommended with a grade of A or B by the United States Preventive Services Task Force, beginning on the date on which coverage for such test is provided pursuant to , the preceding provisions of this paragraph shall not apply.section 1395x(ddd)(1) of this title
